title: BFA++: Hierarchical Best-Feature-Aware Token Prune for Multi-View Vision Language Action Model
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have achieved significant breakthroughs by leveraging Large Vision Language Models (VLMs) to jointly interpret instructions and visual inputs. However, the substantial increase in visual tokens, particularly from multi-view inputs, poses serious challenges to real-time robotic manipulation. Existing acceleration techniques for VLMs, such as token pruning, often result in degraded performance when directly applied to VLA models, as they overlook the relationships between different views and fail to account for the dynamic and task-specific characteristics of robotic operation. To address this, we propose BFA++, a dynamic token pruning framework designed specifically for VLA models. BFA++ introduces a hierarchical pruning strategy guided by two-level importance predictors: an intra-view predictor highlights task-relevant regions within each image to suppress spatial noise, while an inter-view predictor identifies critical camera views throughout different manipulation phases to reduce cross-view redundancy. This design enables efficient token selection while preserving essential visual cues, resulting in improved computational efficiency and higher manipulation success rates. Evaluations on the RoboTwin benchmark and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate that BFA++ consistently outperforms existing methods. BFA++ improves the success rate by about 10% on both the π0 and RDT models, achieving speedup of 1.8X and 1.5X, respectively. Our results highlight that context-sensitive and task-aware token pruning serves as a more effective strategy than full visual processing, enabling faster inference and improved manipulation accuracy in real-world robotic systems.
tldr: The paper introduces BFA++, a hierarchical token pruning framework for VLA models that improves computational efficiency and manipulation success rates by dynamically selecting important visual cues from multi-view inputs, outperforming existing methods on robotic manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了BFA++，一个用于VLA模型的分层令牌修剪框架，通过动态选择多视角输入中的重要视觉线索，提高了计算效率和操作成功率，优于现有的机器人操作方法。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型通过利用大型视觉语言模型 (VLMs) 来联合解释指令和视觉输入，取得了显著突破。然而，视觉Token的大量增加，特别是来自多视角输入时，对实时机器人操作提出了严峻挑战。现有的VLMs加速技术，例如Token剪枝，直接应用于VLA模型时通常会导致性能下降，因为它们忽略了不同视角之间的关系，并且未能考虑到机器人操作的动态和任务特定特征。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了BFA++，一个专为VLA模型设计的动态Token剪枝框架。BFA++引入了一种由两层重要性预测器引导的分层剪枝策略：一个视角内预测器突出显示每个图像中与任务相关的区域以抑制空间噪声，而一个视角间预测器识别整个不同操作阶段的关键相机视角以减少跨视角冗余。这种设计能够高效地选择Token，同时保留必要的视觉线索，从而提高计算效率和更高的操作成功率。在 RoboTwin 基准测试和真实世界机器人任务上的评估表明，BFA++ 始终优于现有方法。BFA++ 将 π0 和 RDT 模型上的成功率分别提高了约 10%，并实现了 1.8 倍和 1.5 倍的加速。我们的结果表明，与完整的视觉处理相比，上下文敏感和任务感知的Token剪枝是一种更有效的策略，可以在真实世界机器人系统中实现更快的推理和更高的操作精度。

title: UniLACT: Depth-Aware RGB Latent Action Learning for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Latent action representations learned from unlabeled videos have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for pretraining vision-language-action (VLA) models without explicit robot action supervision. However, latent actions derived solely from RGB observations primarily encode appearance-driven dynamics and lack explicit 3D geometric structure, which is essential for precise and contact-rich manipulation. To address this limitation, we introduce UniLACT, a transformer-based VLA model that incorporates geometric structure through depth-aware latent pretraining, enabling downstream policies to inherit stronger spatial priors. To facilitate this process, we propose UniLARN, a unified latent action learning framework based on inverse and forward dynamics objectives that learns a shared embedding space for RGB and depth while explicitly modeling their cross-modal interactions. This formulation produces modality-specific and unified latent action representations that serve as pseudo-labels for the depth-aware pretraining of UniLACT. Extensive experiments in both simulation and real-world settings demonstrate the effectiveness of depth-aware unified latent action representations. UniLACT consistently outperforms RGB-based latent action baselines under in-domain and out-of-domain pretraining regimes, as well as on both seen and unseen manipulation tasks.
tldr: The paper introduces UniLACT, a VLA model that incorporates depth information during latent action pretraining to improve performance in manipulation tasks, especially in contact-rich scenarios. It uses a unified latent action learning framework (UniLARN) to learn shared RGB-D embeddings.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 UniLACT，一个 VLA 模型，它在潜在动作预训练期间整合了深度信息，以提高在操作任务中的性能，尤其是在接触丰富的场景中。它使用统一的潜在动作学习框架 (UniLARN) 来学习共享的 RGB-D 嵌入。
summary_zh: 从无标签视频中学习到的潜在动作表征已经成为一种有前景的范例，用于预训练视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型，而无需显式的机器人动作监督。然而，仅从RGB观测推导出的潜在动作主要编码外观驱动的动态，并且缺乏显式的3D几何结构，这对精确且富含接触的操纵至关重要。为了解决这一局限性，我们引入了UniLACT，一种基于Transformer的VLA模型，它通过深度感知的潜在预训练来整合几何结构，从而使下游策略能够继承更强的空间先验。为了促进这个过程，我们提出了UniLARN，一个基于逆向和正向动力学目标的统一潜在动作学习框架，它可以学习RGB和深度的共享嵌入空间，同时显式地建模它们的跨模态交互。这种表述产生了模态特异性和统一的潜在动作表征，这些表征可作为UniLACT深度感知预训练的伪标签。在模拟和真实世界环境中的大量实验证明了深度感知统一潜在动作表征的有效性。在领域内和领域外的预训练机制下，以及在已见和未见的操纵任务中，UniLACT始终优于基于RGB的潜在动作基线。

title: NovaPlan: Zero-Shot Long-Horizon Manipulation via Closed-Loop Video Language Planning
summary: Solving long-horizon tasks requires robots to integrate high-level semantic reasoning with low-level physical interaction. While vision-language models (VLMs) and video generation models can decompose tasks and imagine outcomes, they often lack the physical grounding necessary for real-world execution. We introduce NovaPlan, a hierarchical framework that unifies closed-loop VLM and video planning with geometrically grounded robot execution for zero-shot long-horizon manipulation. At the high level, a VLM planner decomposes tasks into sub-goals and monitors robot execution in a closed loop, enabling the system to recover from single-step failures through autonomous re-planning. To compute low-level robot actions, we extract and utilize both task-relevant object keypoints and human hand poses as kinematic priors from the generated videos, and employ a switching mechanism to choose the better one as a reference for robot actions, maintaining stable execution even under heavy occlusion or depth inaccuracy. We demonstrate the effectiveness of NovaPlan on three long-horizon tasks and the Functional Manipulation Benchmark (FMB). Our results show that NovaPlan can perform complex assembly tasks and exhibit dexterous error recovery behaviors without any prior demonstrations or training. Project page: https://nova-plan.github.io/
tldr: NovaPlan is a zero-shot, hierarchical framework using VLMs and video planning with grounded robot execution for long-horizon manipulation, demonstrating success on complex assembly tasks and error recovery without training.
tldr_zh: NovaPlan是一个零样本的分层框架，它使用视觉语言模型（VLM）和视频规划与接地的机器人执行，用于长期的操作任务。它在复杂的装配任务和错误恢复方面表现出了成功，无需任何训练。
summary_zh: 解决长程任务需要机器人将高层语义推理与低层物理交互相结合。虽然视觉-语言模型（VLMs）和视频生成模型可以分解任务并想象结果，但它们通常缺乏真实世界执行所需的物理基础。我们提出NovaPlan，一个分层框架，它统一了闭环VLM和视频规划与几何基础的机器人执行，用于零样本长程操作。在高层，VLM规划器将任务分解为子目标，并在闭环中监控机器人执行，使系统能够通过自主重新规划从单步失败中恢复。为了计算低层机器人动作，我们从生成的视频中提取并利用任务相关的对象关键点和人手姿态作为运动学先验，并采用切换机制来选择更好的一个作为机器人动作的参考，从而即使在严重遮挡或深度不准确的情况下也能保持稳定的执行。我们在三个长程任务和功能操作基准（FMB）上展示了NovaPlan的有效性。我们的结果表明，NovaPlan可以在没有任何先验演示或训练的情况下执行复杂的组装任务并展示出灵巧的错误恢复行为。项目页面：https://nova-plan.github.io/

title: Recursive Belief Vision Language Model
summary: Current vision-language-action (VLA) models struggle with long-horizon manipulation under partial observability. Most existing approaches remain observation-driven, relying on short context windows or repeated queries to vision-language models (VLMs). This leads to loss of task progress, action repetition under perceptual aliasing, and high inference latency. Semantic reasoning alone is not the primary bottleneck in long-horizon manipulation. Instead, VLAs lack persistent, action-conditioned state representations and exhibit limited temporal and physical reasoning, making them ill-suited for multi-stage control. This paper introduces RB-VLA, a belief-centric architecture trained with self-supervised world-model objectives that maintains a compact latent state encoding task-relevant history, dynamics, and object interactions. Queried once for high-level intent, the VLM provides task specification, while the belief tracks task progress and enables phase-aware, causally grounded control under partial observability without storing raw observations or scaling memory with time. The belief and intent jointly condition a diffusion policy for robust closed-loop execution. RB-VLA outperforms prior VLAs on long-horizon benchmarks, achieving 52.5% and 37.5% higher success on multi-stage pick-and-place and stacking tasks, respectively, compared to π0. It also reduces inference latency by up to 5x relative to baselines and eliminates memory growth across timesteps observed in existing VLAs. Ablations show that the belief module is the primary driver of performance, increasing success rates from 32.5% to 77.5%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of belief-based state representations for long-horizon VLA policies.
tldr: The paper introduces RB-VLA, a belief-centric vision-language-action model leveraging self-supervised world-model objectives for robust long-horizon manipulation under partial observability, outperforming existing VLAs in benchmark tasks with higher success rates and reduced inference latency.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 RB-VLA，一种以置信度为中心的视觉-语言-动作模型，利用自监督的世界模型目标，在部分可观察条件下进行鲁棒的长期操作，在基准测试中优于现有的 VLA 模型，具有更高的成功率和更低的推理延迟。
summary_zh: 当前视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在部分可观察性下的长时程操作中表现不佳。现有的大多数方法仍然是观察驱动的，依赖于短上下文窗口或对视觉-语言模型（VLM）的重复查询。这导致任务进度丢失、感知混淆下的动作重复以及高推理延迟。语义推理本身并非长时程操作的主要瓶颈。相反，VLA缺乏持久的、动作条件的状态表示，并且表现出有限的时间和物理推理能力，使其不适合多阶段控制。本文介绍了一种以置信度为中心的架构RB-VLA，该架构使用自监督世界模型目标进行训练，维护一个紧凑的隐式状态，编码与任务相关的历史、动态和对象交互。VLM仅被查询一次以获取高层意图，提供任务规范，而置信度跟踪任务进度，并允许在部分可观察性下进行阶段感知、因果关系明确的控制，而无需存储原始观察或随时间缩放内存。置信度和意图共同调节扩散策略，以实现鲁棒的闭环执行。在长时程基准测试中，RB-VLA优于先前的VLA，在多阶段的抓取放置和堆叠任务中，成功率分别比π0高52.5%和37.5%。相对于基线，它还将推理延迟降低了高达5倍，并消除了现有VLA中观察到的跨时间步的内存增长。消融实验表明，置信度模块是性能的主要驱动力，将成功率从32.5%提高到77.5%。这些结果证明了基于置信度的状态表示对于长时程VLA策略的有效性。

title: Actor-Curator: Co-adaptive Curriculum Learning via Policy-Improvement Bandits for RL Post-Training
summary: Post-training large foundation models with reinforcement learning typically relies on massive and heterogeneous datasets, making effective curriculum learning both critical and challenging. In this work, we propose ACTOR-CURATOR, a scalable and fully automated curriculum learning framework for reinforcement learning post-training of large language models (LLMs). ACTOR-CURATOR learns a neural curator that dynamically selects training problems from large problem banks by directly optimizing for expected policy performance improvement. We formulate problem selection as a non-stationary stochastic bandit problem, derive a principled loss function based on online stochastic mirror descent, and establish regret guarantees under partial feedback. Empirically, ACTOR-CURATOR consistently outperforms uniform sampling and strong curriculum baselines across a wide range of challenging reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating improved training stability and efficiency. Notably, it achieves relative gains of 28.6% on AIME2024 and 30.5% on ARC-1D over the strongest baseline and up to 80% speedup. These results suggest that ACTOR-CURATOR is a powerful and practical approach for scalable LLM post-training.
tldr: The paper introduces ACTOR-CURATOR, a novel curriculum learning framework for RL post-training of LLMs that dynamically selects training problems, achieving significant performance gains and speedups on challenging reasoning benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了ACTOR-CURATOR，一种新颖的课程学习框架，用于LLM的RL后训练，该框架可以动态选择训练问题，并在具有挑战性的推理基准测试中取得显著的性能提升和加速。
summary_zh: 使用强化学习对大型基础模型进行后训练通常依赖于海量且异构的数据集，这使得有效的课程学习既至关重要又充满挑战。 在这项工作中，我们提出 ACTOR-CURATOR，一个可扩展且完全自动化的课程学习框架，用于大型语言模型 (LLM) 的强化学习后训练。 ACTOR-CURATOR 学习一个神经策展人，通过直接优化预期策略性能的提升，从大型问题库中动态选择训练问题。 我们将问题选择表述为一个非平稳随机bandit问题，基于在线随机镜像下降推导出一个原则性损失函数，并在部分反馈下建立了遗憾保证。 实验表明，ACTOR-CURATOR 在各种具有挑战性的推理基准上始终优于均匀采样和强大的课程基线，证明了其改进的训练稳定性和效率。 值得注意的是，与最强的基线相比，它在 AIME2024 上实现了 28.6% 的相对收益，在 ARC-1D 上实现了 30.5% 的相对收益，并且速度提升高达 80%。 这些结果表明 ACTOR-CURATOR 是一种强大而实用的方法，适用于可扩展的 LLM 后训练。

title: BarrierSteer: LLM Safety via Learning Barrier Steering
summary: Despite the state-of-the-art performance of large language models (LLMs) across diverse tasks, their susceptibility to adversarial attacks and unsafe content generation remains a major obstacle to deployment, particularly in high-stakes settings. Addressing this challenge requires safety mechanisms that are both practically effective and supported by rigorous theory. We introduce BarrierSteer, a novel framework that formalizes response safety by embedding learned non-linear safety constraints directly into the model's latent representation space. BarrierSteer employs a steering mechanism based on Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) to efficiently detect and prevent unsafe response trajectories during inference with high precision. By enforcing multiple safety constraints through efficient constraint merging, without modifying the underlying LLM parameters, BarrierSteer preserves the model's original capabilities and performance. We provide theoretical results establishing that applying CBFs in latent space offers a principled and computationally efficient approach to enforcing safety. Our experiments across multiple models and datasets show that BarrierSteer substantially reduces adversarial success rates, decreases unsafe generations, and outperforms existing methods.
tldr: BarrierSteer uses Control Barrier Functions within the latent space of LLMs to enforce safety constraints during inference, effectively reducing adversarial attacks and unsafe content generation without retraining the model.
tldr_zh: BarrierSteer通过在大语言模型的潜在空间中使用控制障碍函数，在推理过程中强制执行安全约束，从而有效减少对抗性攻击和不安全内容的生成，且无需重新训练模型。
summary_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）在各种任务中表现出最先进的性能，但它们对对抗性攻击和不安全内容生成的敏感性仍然是部署的主要障碍，尤其是在高风险环境中。解决这一挑战需要既具有实践有效性又得到严格理论支持的安全机制。我们引入了BarrierSteer，一种新颖的框架，通过将学习到的非线性安全约束直接嵌入到模型的潜在表示空间中来形式化响应安全性。BarrierSteer采用基于控制屏障函数（CBFs）的引导机制，以高精度高效地检测和防止推理过程中的不安全响应轨迹。通过高效的约束合并强制执行多个安全约束，而无需修改底层LLM参数，BarrierSteer保留了模型原有的能力和性能。我们提供了理论结果，表明在潜在空间中应用CBFs为强制执行安全性提供了一种有原则且计算高效的方法。我们在多个模型和数据集上的实验表明，BarrierSteer显著降低了对抗性成功率，减少了不安全生成，并优于现有方法。

title: From Perception to Action: An Interactive Benchmark for Vision Reasoning
summary: Understanding the physical structure is essential for real-world applications such as embodied agents, interactive design, and long-horizon manipulation. Yet, prevailing Vision-Language Model (VLM) evaluations still center on structure-agnostic, single-turn setups (e.g., VQA), which fail to assess agents' ability to reason about how geometry, contact, and support relations jointly constrain what actions are possible in a dynamic environment. To address this gap, we introduce the Causal Hierarchy of Actions and Interactions (CHAIN) benchmark, an interactive 3D, physics-driven testbed designed to evaluate whether models can understand, plan, and execute structured action sequences grounded in physical constraints. CHAIN shifts evaluation from passive perception to active problem solving, spanning tasks such as interlocking mechanical puzzles and 3D stacking and packing. We conduct a comprehensive study of state-of-the-art VLMs and diffusion-based models under unified interactive settings. Our results show that top-performing models still struggle to internalize physical structure and causal constraints, often failing to produce reliable long-horizon plans and cannot robustly translate perceived structure into effective actions. The project is available at https://social-ai-studio.github.io/CHAIN/.
tldr: The paper introduces CHAIN, a new interactive 3D physics-driven benchmark for evaluating VLMs' ability to reason about physical structure and plan action sequences, revealing their limitations in real-world applications.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个新的交互式3D物理驱动基准测试CHAIN，用于评估VLMs在物理结构推理和规划行动序列方面的能力，并揭示了它们在现实世界应用中的局限性。
summary_zh: 理解物理结构对于具身智能体、交互式设计和长时程操作等实际应用至关重要。然而，目前流行的视觉-语言模型（VLM）评估仍然集中于结构无关、单轮设置（例如，视觉问答），未能评估智能体推理几何、接触和支撑关系如何共同约束动态环境中可能动作的能力。为了弥补这一差距，我们引入了动作与交互的因果层级（CHAIN）基准，这是一个交互式3D、物理驱动的测试平台，旨在评估模型是否能够理解、规划和执行基于物理约束的结构化动作序列。CHAIN将评估从被动感知转变为主动问题解决，涵盖诸如连锁机械谜题以及3D堆叠和打包等任务。我们对最先进的VLM和基于扩散的模型在统一的交互式设置下进行了全面研究。我们的结果表明，性能最佳的模型仍然难以内化物理结构和因果约束，通常无法产生可靠的长时程计划，也无法稳健地将感知到的结构转化为有效的动作。该项目可在https://social-ai-studio.github.io/CHAIN/获取。

title: RAYNOVA: Scale-Temporal Autoregressive World Modeling in Ray Space
summary: World foundation models aim to simulate the evolution of the real world with physically plausible behavior. Unlike prior methods that handle spatial and temporal correlations separately, we propose RAYNOVA, a geometry-agonistic multiview world model for driving scenarios that employs a dual-causal autoregressive framework. It follows both scale-wise and temporal topological orders in the autoregressive process, and leverages global attention for unified 4D spatio-temporal reasoning. Different from existing works that impose strong 3D geometric priors, RAYNOVA constructs an isotropic spatio-temporal representation across views, frames, and scales based on relative Plücker-ray positional encoding, enabling robust generalization to diverse camera setups and ego motions. We further introduce a recurrent training paradigm to alleviate distribution drift in long-horizon video generation. RAYNOVA achieves state-of-the-art multi-view video generation results on nuScenes, while offering higher throughput and strong controllability under diverse input conditions, generalizing to novel views and camera configurations without explicit 3D scene representation. Our code will be released at https://raynova-ai.github.io/.
tldr: RAYNOVA presents a scale-temporal autoregressive world model using Plücker-ray positional encoding for multiview video generation, achieving SOTA results on nuScenes and improved generalization without explicit 3D scene representation.
tldr_zh: RAYNOVA 提出了一个尺度-时间自回归世界模型，使用 Plücker-ray 位置编码进行多视角视频生成，在 nuScenes 上实现了 SOTA 结果，并在没有显式 3D 场景表示的情况下提高了泛化能力。
summary_zh: 世界基础模型旨在模拟真实世界中符合物理规律的演化。与以往分别处理空间和时间相关性的方法不同，我们提出了一种几何无关的多视角世界模型RAYNOVA，用于驾驶场景，它采用双重因果自回归框架。它在自回归过程中遵循尺度和时间拓扑顺序，并利用全局注意力来实现统一的4D时空推理。与现有施加强3D几何先验的工作不同，RAYNOVA基于相对普吕克射线位置编码，构建跨视角、帧和尺度的各向同性时空表示，从而能够稳健地泛化到不同的相机设置和自运动。我们进一步引入了一种循环训练范式，以缓解长时程视频生成中的分布漂移。RAYNOVA在nuScenes数据集上实现了最先进的多视角视频生成结果，同时在各种输入条件下提供了更高的吞吐量和强大的可控性，泛化到新的视角和相机配置，而无需显式的3D场景表示。我们的代码将在https://raynova-ai.github.io/发布。

title: QuantVLA: Scale-Calibrated Post-Training Quantization for Vision-Language-Action Models
summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models unify perception, language, and control for embodied agents but face significant challenges in practical deployment due to rapidly increasing compute and memory demands, especially as models scale to longer horizons and larger backbones. To address these bottlenecks, we introduce QuantVLA, a training-free post-training quantization (PTQ) framework that, to our knowledge, is the first PTQ approach for VLA systems and the first to successfully quantize a diffusion transformer (DiT) action head. QuantVLA incorporates three scale-calibrated components: (1) a selective quantization layout that integerizes all linear layers in both the language backbone and the DiT while keeping attention projections in floating point to preserve the original operator schedule; (2) attention temperature matching, a lightweight per-head scaling mechanism that stabilizes attention logits and is folded into the dequantization scales at inference; and (3) output head balancing, a per-layer residual interface calibration that mitigates post-projection energy drift. The framework requires no additional training, uses only a small unlabeled calibration buffer, and supports integer kernels for low-bit weights and activations while leaving the architecture unchanged. Across representative VLA models on LIBERO, QuantVLA exceeds the task success rates of full-precision baselines, achieves about 70% relative memory savings on the quantized components, and delivers a 1.22x speedup in end-to-end inference latency, providing a practical pathway toward scalable low-bit embodied intelligence under strict compute, memory, and power constraints.
tldr: This paper introduces QuantVLA, a post-training quantization framework for Vision-Language-Action models. It achieves memory savings and speedups without retraining, while maintaining or even exceeding the performance of full-precision models.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了QuantVLA，一个用于视觉-语言-动作模型的训练后量化框架。它在不进行再训练的情况下实现了内存节省和加速，同时保持甚至超过了全精度模型的性能。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型统一了具身智能体的感知、语言和控制，但由于计算和内存需求的快速增长，尤其是在模型扩展到更长的视野和更大的骨干网络时，面临着实际部署中的重大挑战。为了解决这些瓶颈，我们提出了QuantVLA，一种免训练的后训练量化(PTQ)框架，据我们所知，它是第一个用于VLA系统的PTQ方法，也是第一个成功量化扩散Transformer(DiT)动作头的PTQ方法。QuantVLA包含三个尺度校准的组件：（1）一种选择性量化布局，它可以将语言骨干网络和DiT中的所有线性层整数化，同时保持注意力投影采用浮点数，以保留原始的算子调度；（2）注意力温度匹配，一种轻量级的逐头缩放机制，可以稳定注意力logits，并在推理时折叠到反量化尺度中；(3) 输出头平衡，一种逐层残差接口校准，它可以减轻后投影能量漂移。该框架无需额外训练，仅使用一个小型无标签校准缓冲区，并支持用于低比特权重和激活值的整数核，同时保持架构不变。在LIBERO上具有代表性的VLA模型上，QuantVLA超越了全精度基线的任务成功率，在量化组件上实现了约70%的相对内存节省，并提供了1.22倍的端到端推理延迟加速，在严格的计算、内存和功耗约束下，为可扩展的低比特具身智能提供了一条实用的途径。

title: Cooperative-Competitive Team Play of Real-World Craft Robots
summary: Multi-agent deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) has made significant progress in developing intelligent game-playing agents in recent years. However, the efficient training of collective robots using multi-agent RL and the transfer of learned policies to real-world applications remain open research questions. In this work, we first develop a comprehensive robotic system, including simulation, distributed learning framework, and physical robot components. We then propose and evaluate reinforcement learning techniques designed for efficient training of cooperative and competitive policies on this platform. To address the challenges of multi-agent sim-to-real transfer, we introduce Out of Distribution State Initialization (OODSI) to mitigate the impact of the sim-to-real gap. In the experiments, OODSI improves the Sim2Real performance by 20%. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through experiments with a multi-robot car competitive game and a cooperative task in real-world settings.
tldr: The paper presents a multi-agent deep reinforcement learning approach for controlling real-world robots in cooperative and competitive scenarios, introducing Out of Distribution State Initialization (OODSI) to improve sim-to-real transfer.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种多智能体深度强化学习方法，用于在合作和竞争场景中控制真实世界的机器人。它引入了分布外状态初始化（OODSI）来改善模拟到真实的转移。
summary_zh: 近年来，多智能体深度强化学习（RL）在开发智能游戏代理方面取得了显著进展。然而，利用多智能体RL有效训练协作机器人，以及将学习到的策略迁移到现实世界应用，仍然是开放的研究问题。在这项工作中，我们首先开发了一个全面的机器人系统，包括仿真、分布式学习框架和物理机器人组件。然后，我们提出并评估了专为在该平台上高效训练合作和竞争策略而设计的强化学习技术。为了解决多智能体仿真到现实迁移的挑战，我们引入了分布外状态初始化（OODSI）以减轻仿真到现实差距的影响。在实验中，OODSI将Sim2Real性能提高了20%。我们通过多机器人汽车竞速游戏和现实世界环境中的合作任务实验，证明了我们方法的有效性。

title: Notes-to-Self: Scratchpad Augmented VLAs for Memory Dependent Manipulation Tasks
summary: Many dexterous manipulation tasks are non-markovian in nature, yet little attention has been paid to this fact in the recent upsurge of the vision-language-action (VLA) paradigm. Although they are successful in bringing internet-scale semantic understanding to robotics, existing VLAs are primarily "stateless" and struggle with memory-dependent long horizon tasks. In this work, we explore a way to impart both spatial and temporal memory to a VLA by incorporating a language scratchpad. The scratchpad makes it possible to memorize task-specific information, such as object positions, and it allows the model to keep track of a plan and progress towards subgoals within that plan. We evaluate this approach on a split of memory-dependent tasks from the ClevrSkills environment, on MemoryBench, as well as on a challenging real-world pick-and-place task. We show that incorporating a language scratchpad significantly improves generalization on these tasks for both non-recurrent and recurrent models.
tldr: This paper introduces a language scratchpad to enhance Vision-Language-Action models with spatial and temporal memory, improving their performance on memory-dependent manipulation tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种语言暂存器，以增强视觉-语言-动作模型的空间和时间记忆，从而提高它们在依赖记忆的操作任务上的性能。
summary_zh: 许多灵巧操作任务本质上是非马尔可夫的，但在最近视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 范式兴起的大潮中，人们对此关注甚少。虽然它们成功地将互联网规模的语义理解应用于机器人技术，但现有的 VLA 主要都是“无状态的”，难以处理依赖记忆的长期任务。在这项工作中，我们探索了一种通过整合语言暂存器来赋予 VLA 空间和时间记忆的方法。暂存器使得记忆任务特定信息（例如物体位置）成为可能，并允许模型跟踪计划并朝着该计划中的子目标推进。我们在 ClevrSkills 环境中依赖记忆的任务分割、MemoryBench 以及一项具有挑战性的真实世界抓取放置任务上评估了这一方法。我们表明，对于非循环模型和循环模型而言，整合语言暂存器能够显著提高这些任务的泛化能力。

title: GeCo-SRT: Geometry-aware Continual Adaptation for Robotic Cross-Task Sim-to-Real Transfer
summary: Bridging the sim-to-real gap is important for applying low-cost simulation data to real-world robotic systems. However, previous methods are severely limited by treating each transfer as an isolated endeavor, demanding repeated, costly tuning and wasting prior transfer experience. To move beyond isolated sim-to-real, we build a continual cross-task sim-to-real transfer paradigm centered on knowledge accumulation across iterative transfers, thereby enabling effective and efficient adaptation to novel tasks. Thus, we propose GeCo-SRT, a geometry-aware continual adaptation method. It utilizes domain-invariant and task-invariant knowledge from local geometric features as a transferable foundation to accelerate adaptation during subsequent sim-to-real transfers. This method starts with a geometry-aware mixture-of-experts module, which dynamically activates experts to specialize in distinct geometric knowledge to bridge observation sim-to-real gap. Further, the geometry-expert-guided prioritized experience replay module preferentially samples from underutilized experts, refreshing specialized knowledge to combat forgetting and maintain robust cross-task performance. Leveraging knowledge accumulated during iterative transfer, GeCo-SRT method not only achieves 52% average performance improvement over the baseline, but also demonstrates significant data efficiency for new task adaptation with only 1/6 data. We hope this work inspires approaches for efficient, low-cost cross-task sim-to-real transfer.
tldr: The paper introduces GeCo-SRT, a geometry-aware continual learning approach for robotic sim-to-real transfer that accumulates knowledge across tasks using geometric features and prioritized experience replay, achieving improved performance and data efficiency.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了GeCo-SRT，一种几何感知的持续学习方法，用于机器人模拟到真实的迁移，该方法利用几何特征和优先经验重放跨任务积累知识，实现了性能和数据效率的提高。
summary_zh: 弥合从仿真到现实的鸿沟对于将低成本仿真数据应用于真实世界的机器人系统至关重要。然而，以往的方法将每次迁移都视为孤立的工作，需要重复且昂贵的调整，浪费了以往的迁移经验，因此受到了严重限制。为了超越孤立的从仿真到现实，我们构建了一个持续的跨任务从仿真到现实迁移范式，该范式以迭代迁移过程中的知识积累为中心，从而能够有效且高效地适应新的任务。因此，我们提出了GeCo-SRT，一种几何感知的持续适应方法。它利用来自局部几何特征的域不变和任务不变的知识作为可迁移的基础，以加速后续从仿真到现实迁移过程中的适应。该方法首先使用一个几何感知的专家混合模块，该模块动态激活专家以专门处理不同的几何知识，从而弥合观测层面的从仿真到现实的鸿沟。此外，几何专家引导的优先级经验回放模块优先从未充分利用的专家处进行采样，刷新专门知识以对抗遗忘并保持强大的跨任务性能。凭借在迭代迁移过程中积累的知识，GeCo-SRT方法不仅比基线方法实现了平均52%的性能提升，而且还展示了对新任务适应的显著数据效率，仅需1/6的数据。我们希望这项工作能够启发高效、低成本的跨任务从仿真到现实迁移方法。

title: IG-RFT: An Interaction-Guided RL Framework for VLA Models in Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated significant potential for generalist robotic policies; however, they struggle to generalize to long-horizon complex tasks in novel real-world domains due to distribution shifts and the scarcity of high-quality demonstrations. Although reinforcement learning (RL) offers a promising avenue for policy improvement, applying it to real-world VLA fine-tuning faces challenges regarding exploration efficiency, training stability, and sample cost. To address these issues, we propose IG-RFT, a novel Interaction-Guided Reinforced Fine-Tuning system designed for flow-based VLA models. Firstly, to facilitate effective policy optimization, we introduce Interaction-Guided Advantage Weighted Regression (IG-AWR), an RL algorithm that dynamically modulates exploration intensity based on the robot's interaction status. Furthermore, to address the limitations of sparse or task-specific rewards, we design a novel hybrid dense reward function that integrates the trajectory-level reward and the subtask-level reward. Finally, we construct a three-stage RL system comprising SFT, Offline RL, and Human-in-the-Loop RL for fine-tuning VLA models. Extensive real-world experiments on four challenging long-horizon tasks demonstrate that IG-RFT achieves an average success rate of 85.0%, significantly outperforming SFT (18.8%) and standard Offline RL baselines (40.0%). Ablation studies confirm the critical contributions of IG-AWR and hybrid reward shaping. In summary, our work establishes and validates a novel reinforced fine-tuning system for VLA models in real-world robotic manipulation.
tldr: The paper introduces IG-RFT, a novel interaction-guided reinforced fine-tuning system for Vision-Language-Action models, which significantly improves performance on long-horizon robotic manipulation tasks compared to SFT and offline RL baselines via interaction-guided exploration, hybrid reward shaping and a multi-stage RL training approach.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新的交互引导强化微调（IG-RFT）系统，用于视觉-语言-动作模型。通过交互引导探索、混合奖励塑造和多阶段RL训练方法，与SFT和离线RL基线相比，该系统在长时程机器人操作任务上显著提高了性能。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型在通用机器人策略方面展现出巨大的潜力；然而，由于分布偏移和高质量演示的稀缺性，它们难以泛化到新颖现实世界领域中的长时程复杂任务。尽管强化学习 (RL) 为策略改进提供了一条有希望的途径，但将其应用于现实世界的 VLA 模型微调面临探索效率、训练稳定性和样本成本方面的挑战。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种新颖的交互引导式强化微调系统 IG-RFT，专为基于流的 VLA 模型而设计。首先，为了促进有效的策略优化，我们引入了交互引导式优势加权回归 (IG-AWR)，这是一种 RL 算法，可根据机器人的交互状态动态调节探索强度。此外，为了解决稀疏或任务特定奖励的局限性，我们设计了一种新颖的混合密集奖励函数，该函数集成了轨迹层面的奖励和子任务层面的奖励。最后，我们构建了一个包含 SFT、离线 RL 和人机协同 RL 的三阶段 RL 系统，用于微调 VLA 模型。在四个具有挑战性的长时程任务上的大量真实世界实验表明，IG-RFT 实现了平均 85.0% 的成功率，显著优于 SFT (18.8%) 和标准离线 RL 基线 (40.0%)。消融研究证实了 IG-AWR 和混合奖励塑造的关键贡献。总而言之，我们的工作建立并验证了一种用于现实世界机器人操作中 VLA 模型的新型强化微调系统。

title: Strategy-Supervised Autonomous Laparoscopic Camera Control via Event-Driven Graph Mining
summary: Autonomous laparoscopic camera control must maintain a stable and safe surgical view under rapid tool-tissue interactions while remaining interpretable to surgeons. We present a strategy-grounded framework that couples high-level vision-language inference with low-level closed-loop control. Offline, raw surgical videos are parsed into camera-relevant temporal events (e.g., interaction, working-distance deviation, and view-quality degradation) and structured as attributed event graphs. Mining these graphs yields a compact set of reusable camera-handling strategy primitives, which provide structured supervision for learning. Online, a fine-tuned Vision-Language Model (VLM) processes the live laparoscopic view to predict the dominant strategy and discrete image-based motion commands, executed by an IBVS-RCM controller under strict safety constraints; optional speech input enables intuitive human-in-the-loop conditioning. On a surgeon-annotated dataset, event parsing achieves reliable temporal localization (F1-score 0.86), and the mined strategies show strong semantic alignment with expert interpretation (cluster purity 0.81). Extensive ex vivo experiments on silicone phantoms and porcine tissues demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms junior surgeons in standardized camera-handling evaluations, reducing field-of-view centering error by 35.26% and image shaking by 62.33%, while preserving smooth motion and stable working-distance regulation.
tldr: This paper presents a strategy-supervised autonomous laparoscopic camera control system that uses event-driven graph mining to learn camera-handling strategy primitives and a vision-language model for online control, outperforming junior surgeons in camera-handling tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种策略监督的自主腹腔镜相机控制系统，该系统利用事件驱动的图挖掘来学习相机操作策略原语，并使用视觉-语言模型进行在线控制，在相机操作任务中优于初级外科医生。
summary_zh: 自主腹腔镜相机控制需要在快速器械-组织交互下保持稳定且安全的手术视野，同时对外科医生保持可解释性。我们提出了一种基于策略的框架，该框架将高层视觉-语言推理与低层闭环控制相结合。离线状态下，原始手术视频被解析为与相机相关的时序事件（例如，交互、工作距离偏差和视野质量下降），并结构化为带有属性的事件图。挖掘这些图会产生一组紧凑的可重用相机操作策略基元，从而为学习提供结构化监督。在线状态下，微调后的视觉-语言模型 (VLM) 处理实时的腹腔镜视野，以预测主导策略和离散的基于图像的运动指令，这些指令由 IBVS-RCM 控制器在严格的安全约束下执行；可选的语音输入能够实现直观的人在回路调节。在一个外科医生注释的数据集上，事件解析实现了可靠的时序定位（F1 得分为 0.86），并且挖掘的策略与专家的解释表现出很强的语义对齐（聚类纯度为 0.81）。在硅胶模型和猪组织上的大量离体实验表明，所提出的系统在标准化的相机操作评估中优于初级外科医生，视野中心误差减少 35.26%，图像抖动减少 62.33%，同时保持平滑运动和稳定的工作距离调节。

title: Grasp to Act: Dexterous Grasping for Tool Use in Dynamic Settings
summary: Achieving robust grasping with dexterous hands remains challenging, especially when manipulation involves dynamic forces such as impacts, torques, and continuous resistance--situations common in real-world tool use. Existing methods largely optimize grasps for static geometric stability and often fail once external forces arise during manipulation. We present Grasp-to-Act, a hybrid system that combines physics-based grasp optimization with reinforcement-learning-based grasp adaptation to maintain stable grasps throughout functional manipulation tasks. Our method synthesizes robust grasp configurations informed by human demonstrations and employs an adaptive controller that residually issues joint corrections to prevent in-hand slip while tracking the object trajectory. Grasp-to-Act enables robust zero-shot sim-to-real transfer across five dynamic tool-use tasks--hammering, sawing, cutting, stirring, and scooping--consistently outperforming baselines. Across simulation and real-world hardware trials with a 16-DoF dexterous hand, our method reduces translational and rotational in-hand slip and achieves the highest task completion rates, demonstrating stable functional grasps under dynamic, contact-rich conditions.
tldr: The paper presents Grasp-to-Act, a hybrid physics-based and reinforcement learning system for robust dexterous grasping during dynamic tool use, achieving zero-shot sim-to-real transfer across several tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了Grasp-to-Act，一个混合的基于物理和强化学习的系统，用于在动态工具使用过程中实现稳健灵巧的抓取，并在多个任务中实现了零样本的从仿真到现实的迁移。
summary_zh:
摘要：
使用灵巧手实现鲁棒抓取仍然具有挑战性，尤其是在操控涉及动态力（如冲击、扭矩和持续阻力）的情况下，这些情况在现实世界的工具使用中很常见。现有方法主要针对静态几何稳定性优化抓取，并且经常在操作过程中出现外部力时失效。我们提出了一种混合系统Grasp-to-Act，它结合了基于物理的抓取优化和基于强化学习的抓取自适应，以在整个功能性操控任务中保持稳定的抓取。我们的方法综合了受人类演示启发的鲁棒抓取配置，并采用一种自适应控制器，该控制器以残差方式发出关节修正指令，以在跟踪物体轨迹的同时防止手中滑移。Grasp-to-Act能够在五个动态工具使用任务（锤击、锯切、切割、搅拌和舀取）中实现鲁棒的零样本sim-to-real迁移，并始终优于基线方法。通过在具有16个自由度的灵巧手进行的模拟和真实硬件试验，我们的方法减少了平移和旋转方向的手中滑移，并实现了最高的任务完成率，从而证明了在动态、接触丰富的条件下稳定功能性抓取。

title: Generalizing from References using a Multi-Task Reference and Goal-Driven RL Framework
summary: Learning agile humanoid behaviors from human motion offers a powerful route to natural, coordinated control, but existing approaches face a persistent trade-off: reference-tracking policies are often brittle outside the demonstration dataset, while purely task-driven Reinforcement Learning (RL) can achieve adaptability at the cost of motion quality. We introduce a unified multi-task RL framework that bridges this gap by treating reference motion as a prior for behavioral shaping rather than a deployment-time constraint. A single goal-conditioned policy is trained jointly on two tasks that share the same observation and action spaces, but differ in their initialization schemes, command spaces, and reward structures: (i) a reference-guided imitation task in which reference trajectories define dense imitation rewards but are not provided as policy inputs, and (ii) a goal-conditioned generalization task in which goals are sampled independently of any reference and where rewards reflect only task success. By co-optimizing these objectives within a shared formulation, the policy acquires structured, human-like motor skills from dense reference supervision while learning to adapt these skills to novel goals and initial conditions. This is achieved without adversarial objectives, explicit trajectory tracking, phase variables, or reference-dependent inference. We evaluate the method on a challenging box-based parkour playground that demands diverse athletic behaviors (e.g., jumping and climbing), and show that the learned controller transfers beyond the reference distribution while preserving motion naturalness. Finally, we demonstrate long-horizon behavior generation by composing multiple learned skills, illustrating the flexibility of the learned polices in complex scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces a multi-task reinforcement learning framework that combines reference-guided imitation learning with goal-conditioned RL to achieve both natural human-like motion and adaptation to novel goals in humanoid robotics, demonstrating successful transfer and long-horizon behavior composition in a parkour environment.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种多任务强化学习框架，结合参考引导的模仿学习和目标条件强化学习，在人形机器人中实现自然的类人运动和对新目标的适应性，并在跑酷环境中展示了成功的迁移和长时程行为组合。
summary_zh: 从人类动作中学习敏捷人形机器人行为为自然、协调的控制提供了一条有效的途径，但现有方法面临着一个长期存在的权衡：参考轨迹跟踪策略在演示数据集之外通常很脆弱，而纯粹任务驱动的强化学习(RL)虽然可以实现适应性，但会以运动质量为代价。我们引入了一个统一的多任务RL框架，通过将参考动作视为行为塑造的先验，而不是部署时的约束，来弥合这一差距。一个单一的目标条件策略在两个任务上联合训练，这两个任务共享相同的观察和动作空间，但在初始化方案、命令空间和奖励结构上有所不同：(i)参考引导的模仿任务，其中参考轨迹定义了密集的模仿奖励，但未作为策略的输入提供，以及(ii)目标条件的泛化任务，其中目标的采样独立于任何参考轨迹，并且奖励仅反映任务的成功。通过在共享公式中共同优化这些目标，该策略从密集的参考监督中获得结构化的、类人的运动技能，同时学习将这些技能适应于新的目标和初始条件。这是在没有对抗性目标、显式轨迹跟踪、相位变量或依赖参考的推理的情况下实现的。我们在一个具有挑战性的基于盒子的跑酷游乐场上评估该方法，该游乐场需要多种运动行为（例如，跳跃和攀爬），并表明所学习的控制器能够超越参考分布进行泛化，同时保持运动的自然性。最后，我们通过组合多个学习到的技能来展示长时程行为生成，从而说明了所学习策略在复杂场景中的灵活性。

title: Learning Physical Principles from Interaction: Self-Evolving Planning via Test-Time Memory
summary: Reliable object manipulation requires understanding physical properties that vary across objects and environments. Vision-language model (VLM) planners can reason about friction and stability in general terms; however, they often cannot predict how a specific ball will roll on a particular surface or which stone will provide a stable foundation without direct experience. We present PhysMem, a memory framework that enables VLM robot planners to learn physical principles from interaction at test time, without updating model parameters. The system records experiences, generates candidate hypotheses, and verifies them through targeted interaction before promoting validated knowledge to guide future decisions. A central design choice is verification before application: the system tests hypotheses against new observations rather than applying retrieved experience directly, reducing rigid reliance on prior experience when physical conditions change. We evaluate PhysMem on three real-world manipulation tasks and simulation benchmarks across four VLM backbones. On a controlled brick insertion task, principled abstraction achieves 76% success compared to 23% for direct experience retrieval, and real-world experiments show consistent improvement over 30-minute deployment sessions.
tldr: The paper introduces PhysMem, a memory framework that allows VLM robot planners to learn physical principles from interactions at test time without updating model parameters, enabling more reliable object manipulation through hypothesis verification.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为PhysMem的记忆框架，它允许VLM机器人规划器在测试时从交互中学习物理规律，而无需更新模型参数，从而通过假设验证实现更可靠的物体操作。
summary_zh: 可靠的物体操控需要理解物体和环境之间变化的物理特性。视觉-语言模型(VLM)规划器可以泛泛地推理摩擦和稳定性；然而，在没有直接经验的情况下，它们往往无法预测某个特定的球会在特定表面上如何滚动，或者哪块石头能提供稳定的基础。我们提出了PhysMem，一个内存框架，使VLM机器人规划器能够在测试时通过交互学习物理原理，而无需更新模型参数。该系统记录经验，生成候选假设，并通过有针对性的交互进行验证，然后提升已验证的知识以指导未来的决策。一个核心的设计选择是在应用之前进行验证：该系统基于新的观察来测试假设，而不是直接应用检索到的经验，从而减少了在物理条件变化时对先前经验的僵化依赖。我们评估了PhysMem在三个真实世界的操控任务和使用四个VLM骨干网络的模拟基准测试中的表现。在一个受控的砖块插入任务中，有原则的抽象实现了76%的成功率，而直接经验检索的成功率为23%，真实世界的实验表明，在30分钟的部署会话中，性能始终得到提升。

title: Simulation-Ready Cluttered Scene Estimation via Physics-aware Joint Shape and Pose Optimization
summary: Estimating simulation-ready scenes from real-world observations is crucial for downstream planning and policy learning tasks. Regretfully, existing methods struggle in cluttered environments, often exhibiting prohibitive computational cost, poor robustness, and restricted generality when scaling to multiple interacting objects. We propose a unified optimization-based formulation for real-to-sim scene estimation that jointly recovers the shapes and poses of multiple rigid objects under physical constraints. Our method is built on two key technical innovations. First, we leverage the recently introduced shape-differentiable contact model, whose global differentiability permits joint optimization over object geometry and pose while modeling inter-object contacts. Second, we exploit the structured sparsity of the augmented Lagrangian Hessian to derive an efficient linear system solver whose computational cost scales favorably with scene complexity. Building on this formulation, we develop an end-to-end real-to-sim scene estimation pipeline that integrates learning-based object initialization, physics-constrained joint shape-pose optimization, and differentiable texture refinement. Experiments on cluttered scenes with up to 5 objects and 22 convex hulls demonstrate that our approach robustly reconstructs physically valid, simulation-ready object shapes and poses.
tldr: This paper presents a method for estimating simulation-ready scenes from real-world observations, addressing challenges in cluttered environments by jointly optimizing object shape and pose under physical constraints. The method improves robustness and efficiency for downstream planning and policy learning tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种从真实世界观测中估计可用于仿真的场景的方法，通过在物理约束下联合优化物体形状和姿态，解决了拥挤环境中的挑战。该方法提高了鲁棒性和效率，适用于下游的规划和策略学习任务。
summary_zh: 从真实世界观测估计可用于仿真的场景对于下游规划和策略学习任务至关重要。遗憾的是，现有方法在杂乱环境中表现不佳，通常展现出令人望而却步的计算成本、较差的鲁棒性和扩展到多个交互对象时的有限泛化性。我们提出了一种统一的基于优化的公式，用于真实到仿真的场景估计，该公式在物理约束下联合恢复多个刚性物体的形状和姿态。我们的方法建立在两个关键技术创新之上。首先，我们利用了最近提出的形状可微接触模型，其全局可微性允许对物体几何和姿态进行联合优化，同时对对象间接触进行建模。其次，我们利用增广拉格朗日Hessian的结构化稀疏性，推导出一种高效的线性系统求解器，其计算成本随场景复杂性增加而增加缓慢。基于此公式，我们开发了一个端到端的真实到仿真的场景估计流程，该流程集成了基于学习的物体初始化、物理约束的联合形状-姿态优化以及可微纹理细化。在包含多达 5 个对象和 22 个凸包的杂乱场景上的实验表明，我们的方法稳健地重建了物理上有效、可用于仿真的物体形状和姿态。

title: VAUQ: Vision-Aware Uncertainty Quantification for LVLM Self-Evaluation
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) frequently hallucinate, limiting their safe deployment in real-world applications. Existing LLM self-evaluation methods rely on a model's ability to estimate the correctness of its own outputs, which can improve deployment reliability; however, they depend heavily on language priors and are therefore ill-suited for evaluating vision-conditioned predictions. We propose VAUQ, a vision-aware uncertainty quantification framework for LVLM self-evaluation that explicitly measures how strongly a model's output depends on visual evidence. VAUQ introduces the Image-Information Score (IS), which captures the reduction in predictive uncertainty attributable to visual input, and an unsupervised core-region masking strategy that amplifies the influence of salient regions. Combining predictive entropy with this core-masked IS yields a training-free scoring function that reliably reflects answer correctness. Comprehensive experiments show that VAUQ consistently outperforms existing self-evaluation methods across multiple datasets.
tldr: The paper introduces VAUQ, a vision-aware uncertainty quantification framework for LVLM self-evaluation, using an Image-Information Score and core-region masking to improve the reliability of self-evaluation for vision-conditioned predictions.
tldr_zh: 本文提出VAUQ，一种视觉感知的LVLM自评估不确定性量化框架，通过图像信息评分和核心区域掩蔽来提高视觉条件预测的自评估可靠性。
summary_zh: 大型视觉-语言模型（LVLM）经常产生幻觉，限制了它们在现实世界应用中的安全部署。现有的LLM自评估方法依赖于模型评估自身输出正确性的能力，这可以提高部署可靠性；然而，这些方法严重依赖于语言先验，因此不适合评估视觉条件下的预测。我们提出了VAUQ，一个视觉感知的LVLM不确定性量化框架，用于LVLM的自评估，该框架明确地衡量了模型输出对视觉证据的依赖程度。VAUQ引入了图像信息得分（IS），它捕捉了可归因于视觉输入的预测不确定性的减少，以及一种无监督的核心区域掩蔽策略，该策略放大了显著区域的影响。将预测熵与这种核心掩蔽的IS相结合，产生了一个无需训练的评分函数，该函数能够可靠地反映答案的正确性。全面的实验表明，VAUQ在多个数据集上始终优于现有的自评估方法。

title: CrystaL: Spontaneous Emergence of Visual Latents in MLLMs
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable performance by integrating powerful language backbones with large-scale visual encoders. Among these, latent Chain-of-Thought (CoT) methods enable implicit reasoning in continuous hidden states, facilitating seamless vision-language integration and faster inference. However, existing heuristically predefined supervision signals in latent CoT provide limited guidance for preserving critical visual information in intermediate latent states. To address this limitation, we propose CrystaL (Crystallized Latent Reasoning), a single-stage framework with two paths to process intact and corrupted images, respectively. By explicitly aligning the attention patterns and prediction distributions across the two paths, CrystaL crystallizes latent representations into task-relevant visual semantics, without relying on auxiliary annotations or external modules. Extensive experiments on perception-intensive benchmarks demonstrate that CrystaL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving substantial gains in fine-grained visual understanding while maintaining robust reasoning capabilities.
tldr: CrystaL improves visual understanding in MLLMs by aligning attention patterns and prediction distributions of intact and corrupted images within a single-stage framework, leading to better performance on perception-intensive tasks without additional annotations.
tldr_zh: CrystaL通过在单阶段框架内对齐完整图像和损坏图像的注意力模式与预测分布，提升了多模态大型语言模型中的视觉理解能力，从而在感知密集型任务上实现了更好的性能，且无需额外标注。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）通过将强大的语言骨干网络与大规模视觉编码器相结合，取得了显著的性能。其中，潜在思维链（CoT）方法能够在连续的隐藏状态中实现隐式推理，从而促进了无缝的视觉-语言集成和更快的推理速度。然而，现有的潜在CoT中启发式预定义的监督信号，对于在中间潜在状态中保留关键视觉信息的指导有限。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出CrystaL（结晶化潜在推理），这是一个单阶段框架，具有分别处理完整和损坏图像的两条路径。通过显式对齐两条路径间的注意力模式和预测分布，CrystaL无需依赖辅助标注或外部模块，即可将潜在表示结晶为任务相关的视觉语义。在感知密集型基准上的大量实验表明，CrystaL始终优于最先进的基线方法，在精细化视觉理解方面取得了显著提升，同时保持了稳健的推理能力。

title: See and Fix the Flaws: Enabling VLMs and Diffusion Models to Comprehend Visual Artifacts via Agentic Data Synthesis
summary: Despite recent advances in diffusion models, AI generated images still often contain visual artifacts that compromise realism. Although more thorough pre-training and bigger models might reduce artifacts, there is no assurance that they can be completely eliminated, which makes artifact mitigation a highly crucial area of study. Previous artifact-aware methodologies depend on human-labeled artifact datasets, which are costly and difficult to scale, underscoring the need for an automated approach to reliably acquire artifact-annotated datasets. In this paper, we propose ArtiAgent, which efficiently creates pairs of real and artifact-injected images. It comprises three agents: a perception agent that recognizes and grounds entities and subentities from real images, a synthesis agent that introduces artifacts via artifact injection tools through novel patch-wise embedding manipulation within a diffusion transformer, and a curation agent that filters the synthesized artifacts and generates both local and global explanations for each instance. Using ArtiAgent, we synthesize 100K images with rich artifact annotations and demonstrate both efficacy and versatility across diverse applications. Code is available at link.
tldr: This paper introduces ArtiAgent, a system leveraging VLMs and diffusion models to automatically generate artifact-annotated image datasets for training models to mitigate visual artifacts in AI-generated images.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了ArtiAgent，一个利用视觉语言模型和扩散模型来自动生成带有伪影标注的图像数据集的系统，用于训练模型以减轻人工智能生成图像中的视觉伪影。
summary_zh: 尽管扩散模型取得了最新进展，但人工智能生成的图像仍然经常包含影响真实感的视觉伪影。虽然更彻底的预训练和更大的模型可能减少伪影，但不能保证它们能够被完全消除，这使得伪影缓解成为一个至关重要的研究领域。先前的伪影感知方法依赖于人工标注的伪影数据集，这些数据集成本高昂且难以扩展，突显了对一种自动方法来可靠地获取伪影标注数据集的需求。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为ArtiAgent的方法，它可以高效地创建真实图像和注入伪影图像对。它包含三个代理：一个感知代理，用于识别和定位真实图像中的实体和子实体；一个合成代理，通过伪影注入工具，利用扩散变换器中新颖的块状嵌入操作来引入伪影；以及一个管理代理，用于过滤合成的伪影，并为每个实例生成局部和全局解释。使用 ArtiAgent，我们合成了10万张带有丰富伪影注释的图像，并在各种应用中展示了其有效性和多功能性。代码可在链接处获取。

title: SoK: Agentic Skills -- Beyond Tool Use in LLM Agents
summary:
Agentic systems increasingly rely on reusable procedural capabilities, \textit{a.k.a., agentic skills}, to execute long-horizon workflows reliably. These capabilities are callable modules that package procedural knowledge with explicit applicability conditions, execution policies, termination criteria, and reusable interfaces. Unlike one-off plans or atomic tool calls, skills operate (and often do well) across tasks.
  This paper maps the skill layer across the full lifecycle (discovery, practice, distillation, storage, composition, evaluation, and update) and introduces two complementary taxonomies. The first is a system-level set of \textbf{seven design patterns} capturing how skills are packaged and executed in practice, from metadata-driven progressive disclosure and executable code skills to self-evolving libraries and marketplace distribution. The second is an orthogonal \textbf{representation $\times$ scope} taxonomy describing what skills \emph{are} (natural language, code, policy, hybrid) and what environments they operate over (web, OS, software engineering, robotics).
  We analyze the security and governance implications of skill-based agents, covering supply-chain risks, prompt injection via skill payloads, and trust-tiered execution, grounded by a case study of the ClawHavoc campaign in which nearly 1{,}200 malicious skills infiltrated a major agent marketplace, exfiltrating API keys, cryptocurrency wallets, and browser credentials at scale. We further survey deterministic evaluation approaches, anchored by recent benchmark evidence that curated skills can substantially improve agent success rates while self-generated skills may degrade them. We conclude with open challenges toward robust, verifiable, and certifiable skills for real-world autonomous agents.
tldr: This paper provides a Systematization of Knowledge (SoK) for 'agentic skills' in LLM agents, covering their lifecycle, design patterns, taxonomies, security implications (including a real-world attack case study), and evaluation challenges.
tldr_zh: 本文对LLM Agents中的“Agentic Skills”进行了知识体系化 (SoK)，涵盖了它们的生命周期、设计模式、分类、安全影响（包括真实世界的攻击案例研究）和评估挑战。
summary_zh:
自主系统越来越依赖于可重用的程序性能力，即\textit{代理技能}，来可靠地执行长时程工作流。这些能力是可调用的模块，其中封装了具有明确适用性条件、执行策略、终止标准和可重用接口的程序性知识。与一次性的计划或原子工具调用不同，技能可以跨任务运行（并且通常运行良好）。

本文将技能层映射到整个生命周期（发现、实践、提炼、存储、组合、评估和更新），并引入了两个互补的分类法。第一个是系统级的\textbf{七种设计模式}，用于捕捉技能在实践中如何封装和执行，从元数据驱动的渐进式披露和可执行代码技能到自演进库和市场分发。第二个是正交的\textbf{表示$\times$范围}分类法，描述了技能\emph{是}什么（自然语言、代码、策略、混合），以及它们在什么环境中运行（Web、操作系统、软件工程、机器人）。

我们分析了基于技能的代理的安全和治理影响，涵盖了供应链风险、通过技能有效载荷进行的提示注入以及信任分级的执行，并以ClawHavoc活动的案例研究为基础。在该活动中，近1200个恶意技能渗透到一个主要的代理市场，大规模地泄露了API密钥、加密货币钱包和浏览器凭据。我们进一步调查了确定性评估方法，并基于最近的基准证据，即精心策划的技能可以显著提高代理的成功率，而自我生成的技能可能会降低成功率。最后，我们讨论了面向真实世界自主代理的稳健、可验证和可认证技能所面临的公开挑战。

title: PyVision-RL: Forging Open Agentic Vision Models via RL
summary: Reinforcement learning for agentic multimodal models often suffers from interaction collapse, where models learn to reduce tool usage and multi-turn reasoning, limiting the benefits of agentic behavior. We introduce PyVision-RL, a reinforcement learning framework for open-weight multimodal models that stabilizes training and sustains interaction. Our approach combines an oversampling-filtering-ranking rollout strategy with an accumulative tool reward to prevent collapse and encourage multi-turn tool use. Using a unified training pipeline, we develop PyVision-Image and PyVision-Video for image and video understanding. For video reasoning, PyVision-Video employs on-demand context construction, selectively sampling task-relevant frames during reasoning to significantly reduce visual token usage. Experiments show strong performance and improved efficiency, demonstrating that sustained interaction and on-demand visual processing are critical for scalable multimodal agents.
tldr: PyVision-RL introduces a reinforcement learning framework that addresses interaction collapse in agentic vision models by using a novel rollout strategy and accumulative rewards, leading to improved performance and efficiency in image and video understanding tasks.
tldr_zh: PyVision-RL 提出了一个强化学习框架，通过一种新颖的 rollout 策略和累积奖励来解决 agentic 视觉模型中的交互崩溃问题，从而提高图像和视频理解任务的性能和效率。
summary_zh: 面向具身多模态模型的强化学习常常受到交互崩溃的影响，模型倾向于减少工具使用和多轮推理，从而限制了具身行为的优势。我们提出了PyVision-RL，一个用于开源多模态模型的强化学习框架，旨在稳定训练并维持交互。我们的方法结合了过采样-过滤-排序rollout策略和累积工具奖励，以防止崩溃并鼓励多轮工具使用。我们通过统一的训练流程，开发了用于图像理解的PyVision-Image和用于视频理解的PyVision-Video。在视频推理方面，PyVision-Video采用按需上下文构建，在推理过程中选择性地采样与任务相关的帧，从而显著减少视觉token的使用。实验结果展示了卓越的性能和改进的效率，证明了持续交互和按需视觉处理对于可扩展的多模态智能体至关重要。

title: Buffer Matters: Unleashing the Power of Off-Policy Reinforcement Learning in Large Language Model Reasoning
summary: Traditional on-policy Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) frameworks suffer from experience waste and reward homogeneity, which directly hinders learning efficiency on difficult samples during large language models post-training. In this paper, we introduce Batch Adaptation Policy Optimization (BAPO), an off-policy RLVR framework to improve the data efficiency in large language models post-training. It dynamically selects training batches by re-evaluating historically difficult samples and reusing high-quality ones, while holding a lower bound guarantee for policy improvement. Extensive experiments further demonstrate that BAPO achieves an average 12.5% improvement over GRPO across mathematics, planning, and visual reasoning tasks. Crucially, BAPO successfully resolves 40.7% of problems that base models consistently fail to solve.
tldr: The paper introduces Batch Adaptation Policy Optimization (BAPO), an off-policy RLVR framework for improving data efficiency in LLM post-training by dynamically selecting and reusing difficult samples, achieving significant performance gains in reasoning tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种批量自适应策略优化（BAPO）框架，这是一种离线策略RLVR方法，通过动态选择和重用困难样本来提高LLM后训练的数据效率，并在推理任务中取得了显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 传统的可验证奖励在线强化学习(RLVR)框架存在经验浪费和奖励同质性问题，这直接阻碍了大型语言模型后训练过程中对困难样本的学习效率。在本文中，我们提出了一种批量自适应策略优化(BAPO)方法，这是一个离线RLVR框架，旨在提高大型语言模型后训练中的数据效率。它通过重新评估历史上的困难样本并复用高质量样本来动态选择训练批次，同时保持策略改进的下界保证。大量实验进一步表明，在数学、规划和视觉推理任务中，BAPO相比GRPO平均提升了12.5%。关键的是，BAPO成功解决了基础模型始终无法解决的40.7%的问题。

title: AdapTools: Adaptive Tool-based Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks on Agentic LLMs
summary: The integration of external data services (e.g., Model Context Protocol, MCP) has made large language model-based agents increasingly powerful for complex task execution. However, this advancement introduces critical security vulnerabilities, particularly indirect prompt injection (IPI) attacks. Existing attack methods are limited by their reliance on static patterns and evaluation on simple language models, failing to address the fast-evolving nature of modern AI agents. We introduce AdapTools, a novel adaptive IPI attack framework that selects stealthier attack tools and generates adaptive attack prompts to create a rigorous security evaluation environment. Our approach comprises two key components: (1) Adaptive Attack Strategy Construction, which develops transferable adversarial strategies for prompt optimization, and (2) Attack Enhancement, which identifies stealthy tools capable of circumventing task-relevance defenses. Comprehensive experimental evaluation shows that AdapTools achieves a 2.13 times improvement in attack success rate while degrading system utility by a factor of 1.78. Notably, the framework maintains its effectiveness even against state-of-the-art defense mechanisms. Our method advances the understanding of IPI attacks and provides a useful reference for future research.
tldr: This paper introduces AdapTools, a framework for adaptive indirect prompt injection (IPI) attacks on agentic LLMs, demonstrating improved attack success rates and sustained effectiveness against defenses.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了AdapTools，一个针对Agentic LLM的自适应间接提示注入（IPI）攻击框架，该框架展示了更高的攻击成功率，并且在防御机制下仍然有效。
summary_zh: 外部数据服务（例如，模型上下文协议，MCP）的集成使得基于大型语言模型的代理在执行复杂任务时变得越来越强大。然而，这种进步带来了关键的安全漏洞，特别是间接提示注入（IPI）攻击。现有的攻击方法受到其对静态模式的依赖以及在简单语言模型上的评估的限制，无法解决现代人工智能代理快速发展的本质。我们引入 AdapTools，这是一种新型的自适应 IPI 攻击框架，它选择更隐蔽的攻击工具并生成自适应攻击提示，以创建一个严苛的安全评估环境。我们的方法包含两个关键组成部分：（1）自适应攻击策略构建，开发用于提示优化的可迁移对抗策略；（2）攻击增强，识别能够绕过任务相关性防御的隐蔽工具。全面的实验评估表明， AdapTools 在攻击成功率上实现了 2.13 倍的提升，同时使系统效用降低了 1.78 倍。值得注意的是，该框架即使在面对最先进的防御机制时也保持了其有效性。我们的方法增进了对 IPI 攻击的理解，并为未来的研究提供了有用的参考。

title: How Foundational Skills Influence VLM-based Embodied Agents:A Native Perspective
summary: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have shown promise for human-level embodied intelligence. However, existing benchmarks for VLM-driven embodied agents often rely on high-level commands or discretized action spaces, which are non-native settings that differ markedly from real-world control. In addition, current benchmarks focus primarily on high-level tasks and lack joint evaluation and analysis at both low and high levels. To address these limitations, we present NativeEmbodied, a challenging benchmark for VLM-driven embodied agents that uses a unified, native low-level action space. Built on diverse simulated scenes, NativeEmbodied includes three representative high-level tasks in complex scenarios to evaluate overall performance. For more detailed analysis, we further decouple the skills required by complex tasks and construct four types of low-level tasks, each targeting a fundamental embodied skill. This joint evaluation across task and skill granularities enables fine-grained assessment of embodied agents. Experiments with state-of-the-art VLMs reveal clear deficiencies in several fundamental embodied skills, and further analysis shows that these bottlenecks significantly limit performance on high-level tasks. NativeEmbodied highlights key challenges for current VLM-driven embodied agents and provides insights to guide future research.
tldr: The paper introduces NativeEmbodied, a new benchmark for VLM-driven embodied agents with low-level action spaces and a focus on evaluating fundamental embodied skills, revealing limitations of current VLMs.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 NativeEmbodied，这是一个针对 VLM 驱动的具身代理的新基准，它具有低级动作空间，并侧重于评估基本的具身技能，揭示了当前 VLM 的局限性。
summary_zh: 视觉语言模型（VLMs）的最新进展在实现人类水平的具身智能方面展现了希望。然而，现有的基于VLM的具身智能体基准测试往往依赖于高级指令或离散化的动作空间，这些非原生设置与真实世界的控制截然不同。此外，当前的基准测试主要关注于高级任务，并且缺乏在低水平和高水平上的联合评估和分析。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了NativeEmbodied，一个具有挑战性的基于VLM的具身智能体基准测试，它使用统一的原生低水平动作空间。NativeEmbodied建立在多样化的模拟场景之上，包含复杂场景中三个具有代表性的高级任务，用于评估整体性能。为了更详细的分析，我们进一步解耦复杂任务所需的技能，并构建了四种类型的低水平任务，每种任务都针对一项基本的具身技能。这种跨任务和技能粒度的联合评估能够对具身智能体进行细粒度评估。使用最先进VLM的实验表明，在若干基本的具身技能方面存在明显的不足，进一步的分析表明，这些瓶颈显著地限制了高级任务的性能。NativeEmbodied突出了当前基于VLM的具身智能体所面临的关键挑战，并为未来的研究提供了指导。

title: Grounding LLMs in Scientific Discovery via Embodied Actions
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown significant potential in scientific discovery but struggle to bridge the gap between theoretical reasoning and verifiable physical simulation. Existing solutions operate in a passive "execute-then-response" loop and thus lacks runtime perception, obscuring agents to transient anomalies (e.g., numerical instability or diverging oscillations). To address this limitation, we propose EmbodiedAct, a framework that transforms established scientific software into active embodied agents by grounding LLMs in embodied actions with a tight perception-execution loop. We instantiate EmbodiedAct within MATLAB and evaluate it on complex engineering design and scientific modeling tasks. Extensive experiments show that EmbodiedAct significantly outperforms existing baselines, achieving SOTA performance by ensuring satisfactory reliability and stability in long-horizon simulations and enhanced accuracy in scientific modeling.
tldr: The paper introduces EmbodiedAct, a framework grounding LLMs in scientific software through embodied actions in a tight perception-execution loop, enabling improved performance in engineering design and scientific modeling tasks by addressing limitations of passive execution-response approaches.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了EmbodiedAct，一个通过在紧密的感知-执行循环中将LLM与具体行为相结合，从而将LLM应用于科学软件的框架。通过解决被动执行-响应方法的局限性，EmbodiedAct 能够在工程设计和科学建模任务中实现更高的性能。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在科学发现中展现出巨大的潜力，但难以弥合理论推理与可验证物理仿真之间的差距。现有的解决方案以被动的“执行-然后-响应”循环运行，缺乏运行时感知，从而使智能体难以察觉瞬态异常（例如，数值不稳定或发散振荡）。为了解决这个局限性，我们提出了EmbodiedAct，一个通过将LLMs置于具有紧密感知-执行循环的具身行动中，将已建立的科学软件转换为主动具身智能体的框架。我们将EmbodiedAct实例化于MATLAB中，并在复杂的工程设计和科学建模任务上对其进行评估。大量实验表明，EmbodiedAct显著优于现有的基线，通过确保长程仿真中的令人满意的可靠性和稳定性，以及提高科学建模的准确性，实现了SOTA性能。

title: Inner Speech as Behavior Guides: Steerable Imitation of Diverse Behaviors for Human-AI coordination
summary: Effective human-AI coordination requires artificial agents capable of exhibiting and responding to human-like behaviors while adapting to changing contexts. Imitation learning has emerged as one of the prominent approaches to build such agents by training them to mimic human-demonstrated behaviors. However, current methods struggle to capture the inherent diversity and non-Markovian nature of human behavior and lack the ability to steer behavior at inference time. Drawing inspiration from the theory of human cognitive processes, where inner speech guides action selection before execution, we propose MIMIC (Modeling Inner Motivations for Imitation and Control), a framework that uses language as an internal representation of behavioral intent. MIMIC employs the novel use of vision-language models as linguistic scaffolding to train a conditional variational autoencoder capable of generating inner speech from observations. A diffusion-based behavior cloning policy then selects actions conditioned on current observations and the generated inner speech. MIMIC enables fine-grained steering of behavior at inference time by conditioning the agent on behavior-specific speech. Experiments across robotic manipulation tasks and human-AI collaboration games demonstrate that MIMIC significantly enhances both behavior diversity and fidelity to human demonstrations while enabling nuanced behavioral steering without training on additional demonstrations. We open source our code and provide pre-trained MIMIC agents and qualitative demos at: https://mimic-research.github.io.
tldr: The paper introduces MIMIC, a framework that leverages language as an internal representation of behavioral intent for imitation learning, enabling fine-grained behavioral steering and improved diversity in robotic manipulation and human-AI collaboration tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了MIMIC，一个利用语言作为行为意图的内部表示的模仿学习框架，从而能够在机器人操作和人机协作任务中实现细粒度的行为控制和更高的多样性。
summary_zh: 有效的人机协作需要人工智能体能够展现和响应类人行为，并在适应不断变化的环境。模仿学习已成为构建此类智能体的主要方法之一，它通过训练智能体来模仿人类的行为演示。然而，当前的方法难以捕捉人类行为内在的多样性和非马尔可夫性，且缺乏在推理时指导行为的能力。受到人类认知过程理论的启发，该理论认为内部语言在执行前指导行动选择，我们提出了MIMIC（建模模仿和控制的内在动机）框架，该框架使用语言作为行为意图的内部表征。MIMIC创新性地利用视觉-语言模型作为语言脚手架，训练一个条件变分自编码器，能够从观察中生成内部语言。然后，基于扩散的行为克隆策略根据当前的观察和生成的内部语言选择动作。MIMIC通过让智能体基于特定行为的语言进行调整，从而在推理时实现对行为的细粒度指导。跨越机器人操作任务和人机协作游戏的实验表明，MIMIC显著提高了行为多样性和对人类演示的保真度，同时实现了细微的行为指导，而无需在额外的演示上进行训练。 我们已开源代码并提供了预训练的MIMIC智能体和定性演示，网址为：https://mimic-research.github.io。

title: ActionEngine: From Reactive to Programmatic GUI Agents via State Machine Memory
summary:
Existing Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents operate through step-by-step calls to vision language models--taking a screenshot, reasoning about the next action, executing it, then repeating on the new page--resulting in high costs and latency that scale with the number of reasoning steps, and limited accuracy due to no persistent memory of previously visited pages.
  We propose ActionEngine, a training-free framework that transitions from reactive execution to programmatic planning through a novel two-agent architecture: a Crawling Agent that constructs an updatable state-machine memory of the GUIs through offline exploration, and an Execution Agent that leverages this memory to synthesize complete, executable Python programs for online task execution.
  To ensure robustness against evolving interfaces, execution failures trigger a vision-based re-grounding fallback that repairs the failed action and updates the memory.
  This design drastically improves both efficiency and accuracy: on Reddit tasks from the WebArena benchmark, our agent achieves 95% task success with on average a single LLM call, compared to 66% for the strongest vision-only baseline, while reducing cost by 11.8x and end-to-end latency by 2x.
  Together, these components yield scalable and reliable GUI interaction by combining global programmatic planning, crawler-validated action templates, and node-level execution with localized validation and repair.
tldr: ActionEngine uses a two-agent architecture with a state-machine memory to improve the efficiency and accuracy of GUI agents, achieving significantly better performance on web tasks compared to existing methods.
tldr_zh: ActionEngine 采用双代理架构，通过状态机记忆来提高 GUI 代理的效率和准确性，在 Web 任务上的表现明显优于现有方法。
summary_zh:
现有的图形用户界面（GUI）代理通过对视觉语言模型进行逐步调用来操作——拍摄屏幕截图，推理下一步动作，执行该动作，然后在新页面上重复此过程——导致成本高昂和延迟，这些成本和延迟随着推理步骤的数量而增加，并且由于没有对先前访问页面的持久记忆而导致准确性受限。

我们提出ActionEngine，这是一个免训练框架，通过一种新颖的双代理架构，从反应式执行过渡到程序化规划：一个爬虫代理，通过离线探索构建GUI的可更新状态机内存；以及一个执行代理，利用此内存合成完整的、可执行的Python程序，用于在线任务执行。

为了确保针对不断变化的界面的鲁棒性，执行失败会触发基于视觉的重新对齐回退机制，该机制可以修复失败的动作并更新内存。

这种设计极大地提高了效率和准确性：在WebArena基准测试的Reddit任务中，我们的代理以平均单次LLM调用实现了95%的任务成功率，而最强的纯视觉基线为66%，同时成本降低了11.8倍，端到端延迟降低了2倍。

总之，这些组件通过结合全局程序化规划、爬虫验证的动作模板以及具有局部验证和修复功能的节点级执行，实现了可扩展且可靠的GUI交互。

title: Learning to Rewrite Tool Descriptions for Reliable LLM-Agent Tool Use
summary: The performance of LLM-based agents depends not only on the agent itself but also on the quality of the tool interfaces it consumes. While prior work has focused heavily on agent fine-tuning, tool interfaces-including natural language descriptions and parameter schemas-remain largely human-oriented and often become a bottleneck, especially when agents must select from large candidate tool sets. Existing approaches to improving tool interfaces rely on execution traces, which are frequently unavailable in cold-start or privacy-constrained settings, and typically optimize each tool independently, limiting scalability and generalization to unseen tools. We propose Trace-Free+, a curriculum learning framework that progressively transfers supervision from trace-rich settings to trace-free deployment, encouraging the model to abstract reusable interface-usage patterns and tool usage outcomes. To support this approach, we construct a large-scale dataset of high-quality tool interfaces using a structured workflow over a diverse collection of tools. Experiments on StableToolBench and RestBench show consistent gains on unseen tools, strong cross-domain generalization, and robustness as the number of candidate tools scales to over 100, demonstrating that tool interface optimization is a practical and deployable complement to agent fine-tuning.
tldr: The paper introduces Trace-Free+, a curriculum learning framework that rewrites tool descriptions for LLM-based agents, improving their reliability and scalability in tool use, especially in cold-start or privacy-constrained settings. It also creates a large-scale dataset of high-quality tool interfaces.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Trace-Free+，这是一种课程学习框架，用于重写LLM代理的工具描述，提高它们在使用工具时的可靠性和可扩展性，尤其是在冷启动或隐私受限的环境中。它还创建了一个大规模的高质量工具接口数据集。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型（LLM）的智能体的性能不仅取决于智能体本身，还取决于其所使用的工具接口的质量。虽然先前的工作主要集中在智能体的微调上，但工具接口，包括自然语言描述和参数模式，仍然主要面向人类，并且经常成为瓶颈，尤其是在智能体必须从大型候选工具集中进行选择时。现有的改进工具接口的方法依赖于执行轨迹，但在冷启动或隐私受限的环境中，执行轨迹通常不可用，并且通常独立优化每个工具，从而限制了可扩展性以及对未见工具的泛化能力。我们提出了Trace-Free+，一个课程学习框架，它逐步地将监督从轨迹丰富的设置转移到无轨迹部署，鼓励模型提取可重用的接口使用模式和工具使用结果。为了支持这种方法，我们使用在多样化工具集合上的结构化工作流程构建了一个大规模的高质量工具接口数据集。在StableToolBench和RestBench上的实验表明，在未见工具上取得了持续的收益，强大的跨领域泛化能力，以及当候选工具的数量扩展到100多个时的稳健性，证明了工具接口优化是智能体微调的一个实用且可部署的补充。

title: Implicit Intelligence -- Evaluating Agents on What Users Don't Say
summary: Real-world requests to AI agents are fundamentally underspecified. Natural human communication relies on shared context and unstated constraints that speakers expect listeners to infer. Current agentic benchmarks test explicit instruction-following but fail to evaluate whether agents can reason about implicit requirements spanning accessibility needs, privacy boundaries, catastrophic risks, and contextual constraints. We present Implicit Intelligence, an evaluation framework testing whether AI agents can move beyond prompt-following to become genuine goal-fulfillers, paired with Agent-as-a-World (AaW), a harness where interactive worlds are defined in human-readable YAML files and simulated by language models. Our scenarios feature apparent simplicity in user requests, hidden complexity in correct solutions, and discoverability of constraints through environmental exploration. Evaluating 16 frontier and open-weight models across 205 scenarios, we find that even the best-performing model achieves only 48.3% scenario pass rate, revealing substantial room for improvement in bridging the gap between literal instruction-following and human-like contextual reasoning.
tldr: This paper introduces a new evaluation framework, Implicit Intelligence, to assess AI agents' ability to infer unstated user needs and constraints, revealing a significant gap between literal instruction-following and human-like contextual reasoning in existing models.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个新的评估框架，即隐式智能，用于评估人工智能代理推断用户未明确说明的需求和约束的能力。评估结果表明，现有模型在字面指令遵循和类人情境推理之间存在显著差距。
summary_zh: 针对人工智能代理的现实世界请求具有根本性的欠规范性。自然的人类交流依赖于共享的语境和未明确说明的约束，说话者期望听者能够推断出来。当前的代理基准测试仅评估明确的指令遵从性，而无法评估代理是否能够推理隐含的需求，这些需求涵盖可访问性需求、隐私边界、灾难性风险和语境约束。我们提出了“隐式智能”，一个评估框架，用于测试人工智能代理是否能够超越提示遵循，成为真正的目标实现者，并将其与“代理即世界”（AaW）相结合，AaW是一种利用人类可读YAML文件定义交互式世界并由语言模型进行模拟的工具。我们的场景具有用户请求表面上的简单性、正确解决方案中隐藏的复杂性，以及通过环境探索来发现约束的能力。我们评估了跨205个场景的16个前沿和开放权重模型，发现即使是表现最佳的模型也仅达到48.3%的场景通过率，表明在弥合字面指令遵从和类人语境推理之间的差距方面仍有很大的改进空间。

title: Diffusion Modulation via Environment Mechanism Modeling for Planning
summary: Diffusion models have shown promising capabilities in trajectory generation for planning in offline reinforcement learning (RL). However, conventional diffusion-based planning methods often fail to account for the fact that generating trajectories in RL requires unique consistency between transitions to ensure coherence in real environments. This oversight can result in considerable discrepancies between the generated trajectories and the underlying mechanisms of a real environment. To address this problem, we propose a novel diffusion-based planning method, termed as Diffusion Modulation via Environment Mechanism Modeling (DMEMM). DMEMM modulates diffusion model training by incorporating key RL environment mechanisms, particularly transition dynamics and reward functions. Experimental results demonstrate that DMEMM achieves state-of-the-art performance for planning with offline reinforcement learning.
tldr: This paper introduces DMEMM, a diffusion-based planning method that incorporates environment dynamics and reward functions into the diffusion model training process, achieving state-of-the-art performance in offline reinforcement learning planning.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种名为DMEMM的扩散规划方法，该方法将环境动力学和奖励函数融入扩散模型训练过程中，在离线强化学习规划中实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 扩散模型在离线强化学习（RL）的轨迹生成规划方面展现出了可喜的能力。然而，传统的基于扩散的规划方法通常未能考虑到在RL中生成轨迹需要过渡之间具有独一无二的一致性，以确保在真实环境中的连贯性。这种疏忽可能导致生成的轨迹与真实环境的底层机制之间存在显著差异。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新颖的基于扩散的规划方法，称为基于环境机制建模的扩散调制（DMEMM）。DMEMM通过整合关键的RL环境机制，特别是过渡动态和奖励函数，来调节扩散模型的训练。实验结果表明，DMEMM在离线强化学习的规划方面实现了最先进的性能。

title: Agentic AI for Scalable and Robust Optical Systems Control
summary: We present AgentOptics, an agentic AI framework for high-fidelity, autonomous optical system control built on the Model Context Protocol (MCP). AgentOptics interprets natural language tasks and executes protocol-compliant actions on heterogeneous optical devices through a structured tool abstraction layer. We implement 64 standardized MCP tools across 8 representative optical devices and construct a 410-task benchmark to evaluate request understanding, role-aware responses, multi-step coordination, robustness to linguistic variation, and error handling. We assess two deployment configurations--commercial online LLMs and locally hosted open-source LLMs--and compare them with LLM-based code generation baselines. AgentOptics achieves 87.7%--99.0% average task success rates, significantly outperforming code-generation approaches, which reach up to 50% success. We further demonstrate broader applicability through five case studies extending beyond device-level control to system orchestration, monitoring, and closed-loop optimization. These include DWDM link provisioning and coordinated monitoring of coherent 400 GbE and analog radio-over-fiber (ARoF) channels; autonomous characterization and bias optimization of a wideband ARoF link carrying 5G fronthaul traffic; multi-span channel provisioning with launch power optimization; closed-loop fiber polarization stabilization; and distributed acoustic sensing (DAS)-based fiber monitoring with LLM-assisted event detection. These results establish AgentOptics as a scalable, robust paradigm for autonomous control and orchestration of heterogeneous optical systems.
tldr: The paper introduces AgentOptics, an agentic AI framework for autonomous optical system control that uses LLMs to interpret natural language tasks and execute actions on optical devices, achieving high success rates on a benchmark and demonstrating broader applicability through several case studies.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 AgentOptics，一个用于自主光学系统控制的 Agentic AI 框架，它使用 LLM 来解释自然语言任务并在光学设备上执行操作，在一个基准测试中实现了很高的成功率，并通过几个案例研究展示了更广泛的适用性。
summary_zh: 我们提出了 AgentOptics，一种基于模型上下文协议（MCP）构建的智能体AI框架，用于高保真、自主的光学系统控制。AgentOptics 解释自然语言任务，并通过结构化的工具抽象层在异构光学设备上执行符合协议的操作。我们在 8 种代表性光学设备上实现了 64 个标准化 MCP 工具，并构建了一个包含 410 个任务的基准来评估请求理解、角色感知响应、多步骤协调、对语言变异的鲁棒性以及错误处理能力。我们评估了两种部署配置——商业在线 LLM 和本地托管的开源 LLM——并将它们与基于 LLM 的代码生成基线进行比较。AgentOptics 实现了 87.7%--99.0% 的平均任务成功率，显著优于代码生成方法，后者最高达到 50% 的成功率。 我们进一步通过五个案例研究展示了更广泛的适用性，这些案例超越了设备级控制，扩展到系统编排、监控和闭环优化。这些案例包括 DWDM 链路配置以及对相干 400 GbE 和模拟射频光纤（ARoF）信道的协同监控；对承载 5G 前传业务的宽带 ARoF 链路的自主特征分析和偏置优化；具有发射功率优化的多跨段信道配置；闭环光纤极化稳定；以及基于分布式声波传感（DAS）的光纤监控和LLM辅助的事件检测。这些结果确立了 AgentOptics 作为一种可扩展、鲁棒的范例，用于异构光学系统的自主控制和编排。

title: StoryTailor:A Zero-Shot Pipeline for Action-Rich Multi-Subject Visual Narratives
summary: Generating multi-frame, action-rich visual narratives without fine-tuning faces a threefold tension: action text faithfulness, subject identity fidelity, and cross-frame background continuity. We propose StoryTailor, a zero-shot pipeline that runs on a single RTX 4090 (24 GB) and produces temporally coherent, identity-preserving image sequences from a long narrative prompt, per-subject references, and grounding boxes. Three synergistic modules drive the system: Gaussian-Centered Attention (GCA) to dynamically focus on each subject core and ease grounding-box overlaps; Action-Boost Singular Value Reweighting (AB-SVR) to amplify action-related directions in the text embedding space; and Selective Forgetting Cache (SFC) that retains transferable background cues, forgets nonessential history, and selectively surfaces retained cues to build cross-scene semantic ties. Compared with baseline methods, experiments show that CLIP-T improves by up to 10-15%, with DreamSim lower than strong baselines, while CLIP-I stays in a visually acceptable, competitive range. With matched resolution and steps on a 24 GB GPU, inference is faster than FluxKontext. Qualitatively, StoryTailor delivers expressive interactions and evolving yet stable scenes.
tldr: The paper introduces StoryTailor, a zero-shot pipeline for generating action-rich, multi-subject visual narratives that preserves identity and maintains background continuity, running efficiently on a single RTX 4090.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为 StoryTailor 的零样本流程，用于生成富含动作、多主体的视觉叙事，能在保留人物身份和保持背景连续性的同时，在一张 RTX 4090 上高效运行。
summary_zh: 在不进行微调的情况下生成多帧、动作丰富的视觉叙事面临三重挑战：动作文本的忠实性、主体身份的保真度以及跨帧背景的连贯性。我们提出 StoryTailor，这是一个零样本流程，可以在单张 RTX 4090 (24 GB) 上运行，并从长叙事提示、每个主体的参考以及 grounding box 中生成时间上连贯、身份保持的图像序列。三个协同模块驱动该系统：高斯中心化注意力 (GCA) 用于动态关注每个主体的核心并缓解 grounding box 的重叠；动作增强奇异值重加权 (AB-SVR) 用于放大文本嵌入空间中与动作相关的方向；以及选择性遗忘缓存 (SFC) 用于保留可迁移的背景线索，遗忘非必要的历史信息，并选择性地提取保留的线索以建立跨场景的语义联系。与基线方法相比，实验表明 CLIP-T 提高了 10-15%，DreamSim 低于强基线，而 CLIP-I 保持在视觉上可接受的竞争范围内。在 24 GB GPU 上匹配分辨率和步数的情况下，推理速度比 FluxKontext 更快。从定性角度来看，StoryTailor 能够提供富有表现力的交互和演化但稳定的场景。

title: UFO: Unifying Feed-Forward and Optimization-based Methods for Large Driving Scene Modeling
summary: Dynamic driving scene reconstruction is critical for autonomous driving simulation and closed-loop learning. While recent feed-forward methods have shown promise for 3D reconstruction, they struggle with long-range driving sequences due to quadratic complexity in sequence length and challenges in modeling dynamic objects over extended durations. We propose UFO, a novel recurrent paradigm that combines the benefits of optimization-based and feed-forward methods for efficient long-range 4D reconstruction. Our approach maintains a 4D scene representation that is iteratively refined as new observations arrive, using a visibility-based filtering mechanism to select informative scene tokens and enable efficient processing of long sequences. For dynamic objects, we introduce an object pose-guided modeling approach that supports accurate long-range motion capture. Experiments on the Waymo Open Dataset demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms both per-scene optimization and existing feed-forward methods across various sequence lengths. Notably, our approach can reconstruct 16-second driving logs within 0.5 second while maintaining superior visual quality and geometric accuracy.
tldr: The paper introduces UFO, a recurrent method combining optimization and feed-forward techniques for efficient and accurate long-range 4D reconstruction of driving scenes, outperforming existing methods on the Waymo Open Dataset.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为UFO的循环方法，它结合了优化和前馈技术，用于高效、准确地进行长距离驾驶场景的4D重建，并在Waymo开放数据集上优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 动态驾驶场景重建对于自动驾驶仿真和闭环学习至关重要。虽然最近的前馈方法在3D重建方面展现了潜力，但由于序列长度的二次复杂度以及在较长时间内对动态对象建模的挑战，它们难以处理长距离驾驶序列。我们提出了一种名为UFO的新型循环范式，它结合了基于优化的方法和前馈方法的优点，从而实现高效的长距离4D重建。我们的方法维护一个4D场景表示，随着新观测值的到来迭代地进行细化，并使用基于可见性的滤波机制来选择信息丰富的场景标记，从而能够高效地处理长序列。对于动态对象，我们引入了一种对象姿态引导的建模方法，以支持精确的长距离运动捕捉。在Waymo Open Dataset上的实验表明，我们的方法在各种序列长度上都显著优于场景优化和现有的前馈方法。值得注意的是，我们的方法可以在0.5秒内重建16秒的驾驶日志，同时保持卓越的视觉质量和几何精度。

title: LongVideo-R1: Smart Navigation for Low-cost Long Video Understanding
summary: This paper addresses the critical and underexplored challenge of long video understanding with low computational budgets. We propose LongVideo-R1, an active, reasoning-equipped multimodal large language model (MLLM) agent designed for efficient video context navigation, avoiding the redundancy of exhaustive search. At the core of LongVideo-R1 lies a reasoning module that leverages high-level visual cues to infer the most informative video clip for subsequent processing. During inference, the agent initiates traversal from top-level visual summaries and iteratively refines its focus, immediately halting the exploration process upon acquiring sufficient knowledge to answer the query. To facilitate training, we first extract hierarchical video captions from CGBench, a video corpus with grounding annotations, and guide GPT-5 to generate 33K high-quality chain-of-thought-with-tool trajectories. The LongVideo-R1 agent is fine-tuned upon the Qwen-3-8B model through a two-stage paradigm: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by reinforcement learning (RL), where RL employs a specifically designed reward function to maximize selective and efficient clip navigation. Experiments on multiple long video benchmarks validate the effectiveness of name, which enjoys superior tradeoff between QA accuracy and efficiency. All curated data and source code are provided in the supplementary material and will be made publicly available. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/qiujihao19/LongVideo-R1
tldr: The paper introduces LongVideo-R1, a reasoning-equipped MLLM agent that efficiently navigates long videos to answer questions by selectively processing informative clips, trained using a novel two-stage SFT and RL paradigm.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了LongVideo-R1，一种具备推理能力的多模态大语言模型智能体，通过选择性地处理信息丰富的片段来高效地导航长视频以回答问题，该智能体通过新颖的两阶段SFT和RL范式进行训练。
