summary_zh: 随着机器人研究不断提出人机交互中碰撞避免策略，但受限环境和未来人形系统的现实情况使得接触不可避免。为了减轻受伤风险，通常使用能量约束控制方法，这些方法通常依赖于从EN ISO 10218-2:2025 中的钝性冲击数据导出的安全阈值。然而，该数据集并未涵盖边缘或尖锐物体的碰撞。 在缺乏涵盖多样接触场景的可扩展、以临床为基础的数据集的情况下，安全验证仍然有限。先前的研究已通过评估基于替代物的速度和质量限制奠定了基础，该研究涵盖了各种几何形状，并侧重于垂直冲击。本研究通过在无约束碰撞中包含剪切接触场景来扩展这些数据集，揭示了碰撞角度对损伤结果的显著影响。值得注意的是，无约束剪切接触造成的损伤少于垂直接触造成的损伤。通过重新评估所有先前的猪替代物数据，我们建立了跨几何形状和接触类型的能量阈值，形成了第一个基于能量的损伤保护数据库。这使得能够开发有意义的能量限制控制器，从而确保在各种现实碰撞事件中的安全性。

title: Onboard-Targeted Segmentation of Straylight in Space Camera Sensors
summary: This study details an artificial intelligence (AI)-based methodology for the semantic segmentation of space camera faults. Specifically, we address the segmentation of straylight effects induced by solar presence around the camera's Field of View (FoV). Anomalous images are sourced from our published dataset. Our approach emphasizes generalization across diverse flare textures, leveraging pre-training on a public dataset (Flare7k++) including flares in various non-space contexts to mitigate the scarcity of realistic space-specific data. A DeepLabV3 model with MobileNetV3 backbone performs the segmentation task. The model design targets deployment in spacecraft resource-constrained hardware. Finally, based on a proposed interface between our model and the onboard navigation pipeline, we develop custom metrics to assess the model's performance in the system-level context.
tldr: This paper introduces an AI-based method for segmenting straylight in space camera images, using a DeepLabV3 model pre-trained on non-space flare datasets for generalization and designed for onboard deployment.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种基于人工智能的航天相机图像中杂散光分割方法，该方法使用在非航天耀斑数据集上预训练的 DeepLabV3 模型以实现泛化，并设计用于星载部署。
summary_zh: 本研究详细介绍了一种基于人工智能（AI）的空间相机故障语义分割方法。具体而言，我们致力于分割由太阳光在相机视场（FoV）附近引起的杂散光效应。异常图像来源于我们已发布的公开数据集。我们的方法强调在各种耀斑纹理上的泛化能力，利用在包括各种非空间环境下的耀斑的公开数据集（Flare7k++）上的预训练来缓解真实空间专用数据的稀缺性。采用带有MobileNetV3骨干网络的DeepLabV3模型执行分割任务。该模型设计旨在部署于航天器资源受限的硬件中。最后，基于我们提出的模型与在轨导航流水线之间的接口，我们开发了定制的指标来评估模型在系统级环境中的性能。

title: Optimizing Occupancy Sensor Placement in Smart Environments
summary: Understanding the locations of occupants in a commercial built environment is critical for realizing energy savings by delivering lighting, heating, and cooling only where it is needed. The key to achieving this goal is being able to recognize zone occupancy in real time, without impeding occupants' activities or compromising privacy. While low-resolution, privacy-preserving time-of-flight (ToF) sensor networks have demonstrated good performance in zone counting, the performance depends on careful sensor placement. To address this issue, we propose an automatic sensor placement method that determines optimal sensor layouts for a given number of sensors, and can predict the counting accuracy of such a layout. In particular, given the geometric constraints of an office environment, we simulate a large number of occupant trajectories. We then formulate the sensor placement problem as an integer linear programming (ILP) problem and solve it with the branch and bound method. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method based on simulations of several different office environments.
tldr: This paper proposes an automatic sensor placement method to optimize occupancy detection in smart environments using time-of-flight sensors and integer linear programming, aiming for energy savings.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种自动传感器放置方法，通过使用飞行时间传感器和整数线性规划来优化智能环境中的占用检测，旨在节能。
summary_zh: 理解商业建筑环境中人员的定位对于实现节能至关重要，因为它可以只在需要的地方提供照明、供暖和制冷。实现这一目标的关键在于能够实时识别区域占用情况，同时不影响人员的活动或损害隐私。尽管低分辨率、保护隐私的飞行时间（ToF）传感器网络已在区域计数方面表现出良好的性能，但其性能取决于精心的传感器布置。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种自动传感器布置方法，该方法可以确定给定数量传感器的最佳传感器布局，并可以预测这种布局的计数精度。具体而言，在给定办公环境的几何约束条件下，我们模拟了大量人员轨迹。然后，我们将传感器布置问题表述为一个整数线性规划（ILP）问题，并使用分支定界法求解。我们基于几个不同办公环境的模拟，证明了所提出方法的有效性。

title: Progressive Per-Branch Depth Optimization for DEFOM-Stereo and SAM3 Joint Analysis in UAV Forestry Applications
summary: Accurate per-branch 3D reconstruction is a prerequisite for autonomous UAV-based tree pruning; however, dense disparity maps from modern stereo matchers often remain too noisy for individual branch analysis in complex forest canopies. This paper introduces a progressive pipeline integrating DEFOM-Stereo foundation-model disparity estimation, SAM3 instance segmentation, and multi-stage depth optimization to deliver robust per-branch point clouds. Starting from a naive baseline, we systematically identify and resolve three error families through successive refinements. Mask boundary contamination is first addressed through morphological erosion and subsequently refined via a skeleton-preserving variant to safeguard thin-branch topology. Segmentation inaccuracy is then mitigated using LAB-space Mahalanobis color validation coupled with cross-branch overlap arbitration. Finally, depth noise - the most persistent error source - is initially reduced by outlier removal and median filtering, before being superseded by a robust five-stage scheme comprising MAD global detection, spatial density consensus, local MAD filtering, RGB-guided filtering, and adaptive bilateral filtering. Evaluated on 1920x1080 stereo imagery of Radiata pine (Pinus radiata) acquired with a ZED Mini camera (63 mm baseline) from a UAV in Canterbury, New Zealand, the proposed pipeline reduces the average per-branch depth standard deviation by 82% while retaining edge fidelity. The result is geometrically coherent 3D point clouds suitable for autonomous pruning tool positioning. All code and processed data are publicly released to facilitate further UAV forestry research.
tldr: This paper presents a pipeline for generating accurate per-branch 3D point clouds from UAV stereo imagery for forestry applications, specifically autonomous pruning. It combines DEFOM-Stereo, SAM3, and a multi-stage depth optimization scheme to achieve robust and geometrically coherent reconstructions.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种从无人机立体图像中生成精确的逐枝3D点云的流程，用于林业应用，特别是自主修剪。它结合了DEFOM-Stereo、SAM3和一个多阶段深度优化方案，以实现鲁棒和几何一致的重建。
summary_zh: 精确的单枝条三维重建是基于无人机的自主树木修剪的前提；然而，现代立体匹配器生成的密集视差图通常噪声过大，难以用于复杂森林冠层中的单枝条分析。本文提出了一种渐进式流程，集成了DEFOM-Stereo基础模型的视差估计、SAM3实例分割和多阶段深度优化，以生成稳健的单枝条点云。从一个朴素的基线开始，我们通过连续的改进系统地识别并解决了三个误差族。首先，通过形态学腐蚀解决掩模边界污染，随后通过保留骨架的变体进行细化，以保护细枝条拓扑结构。然后，通过LAB颜色空间马氏距离验证以及跨枝条重叠仲裁来缓解分割不准确性。最后，深度噪声——最持久的误差来源——最初通过离群值剔除和中值滤波来减少，然后被一个稳健的五阶段方案所取代，该方案包括MAD全局检测、空间密度一致性、局部MAD滤波、RGB引导滤波和自适应双边滤波。在利用ZED Mini相机（63毫米基线）从新西兰坎特伯雷的无人机上获取的Radiata松树（辐射松）的1920x1080立体图像上进行评估，所提出的流程将单枝条的平均深度标准差降低了82%，同时保持了边缘保真度。结果是几何上连贯的三维点云，适用于自主修剪工具的定位。所有代码和处理后的数据均已公开发布，以促进进一步的无人机林业研究。

title: Bikelution: Federated Gradient-Boosting for Scalable Shared Micro-Mobility Demand Forecasting
summary: The rapid growth of dockless bike-sharing systems has generated massive spatio-temporal datasets useful for fleet allocation, congestion reduction, and sustainable mobility. Bike demand, however, depends on several external factors, making traditional time-series models insufficient. Centralized Machine Learning (CML) yields high-accuracy forecasts but raises privacy and bandwidth issues when data are distributed across edge devices. To overcome these limitations, we propose Bikelution, an efficient Federated Learning (FL) solution based on gradient-boosted trees that preserves privacy while delivering accurate mid-term demand forecasts up to six hours ahead. Experiments on three real-world BSS datasets show that Bikelution is comparable to its CML-based variant and outperforms the current state-of-the-art. The results highlight the feasibility of privacy-aware demand forecasting and outline the trade-offs between FL and CML approaches.
tldr: The paper introduces Bikelution, a federated learning approach using gradient-boosted trees for predicting bike-sharing demand, achieving performance comparable to centralized methods while preserving privacy.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了Bikelution，一种使用梯度提升树的联邦学习方法，用于预测共享单车需求，在保护隐私的同时实现了与集中式方法相当的性能。
summary_zh:
摘要：
无桩共享单车系统的快速发展产生了大量的时空数据集，可用于车队分配、拥堵缓解和可持续交通。然而，单车需求依赖于多种外部因素，使得传统时间序列模型捉襟见肘。集中式机器学习（CML）能够产生高精度的预测，但当数据分布在边缘设备上时，会引发隐私和带宽问题。为了克服这些局限性，我们提出Bikelution，一种基于梯度提升树的高效联邦学习（FL）解决方案，在保护隐私的同时，提供准确的中期需求预测，提前预测时间可达六小时。在三个真实世界共享单车系统数据集上的实验表明，Bikelution与基于CML的变体相当，并优于当前最先进的方法。结果突显了隐私感知需求预测的可行性，并概述了联邦学习和集中式机器学习方法之间的权衡。

title: Computer-Aided Design of Rational Motions for 4R and 6R Spatial Mechanism Synthesis
summary: This paper focuses on geometric methods for generating rational motions used in the design of single-loop rational linkages, 1-degree-of-freedom mechanisms that can execute prescribed spatial tasks. Building on established rational motion synthesis methods, we introduce a new interpolation scheme for seven 3D points based on cubic quaternionic Bezier curves. The resulting motion admits factorization, i.e. the synthesis of a spatial six-bar mechanism whose tool frame passes the specified seven points. To support engineering practice, we provide open-source CAD tools that implement also the other methods and provide fast visual evaluation of motion generation and mechanism synthesis.
tldr: The paper introduces a CAD tool that generates rational motions for 4R and 6R spatial mechanisms using a cubic quaternionic Bezier curve interpolation scheme for 3D point-based motion synthesis, with open-source availability.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个CAD工具，通过使用三次四元数Bezier曲线插值方案进行3D点运动合成，为4R和6R空间机构生成有理运动，并提供开源版本。
summary_zh: 本文着重研究用于设计单环有理连杆机构的几何方法，该连杆机构是一种具有一度自由度的机构，可以执行指定的空间任务。在已建立的有理运动综合方法的基础上，我们介绍了一种基于三次四元数Bezier曲线的七个3D点的新插值方案。得到的运动允许分解，即综合出一个工具坐标系经过指定的七个点的空间六杆机构。为了支持工程实践，我们提供了开源CAD工具，这些工具也实现了其他方法，并可对运动生成和机构综合进行快速的视觉评估。

title: Conflict-Based Search for Multi-Agent Path Finding with Elevators
summary: This paper investigates a problem called Multi-Agent Path Finding with Elevators (MAPF-E), which seeks conflict-free paths for multiple agents from their start to goal locations that may locate on different floors, and the agents can use elevators to travel between floors. The existence of elevators complicates the interaction among the agents and introduces new challenges to the planning. On the one hand, elevators can cause many conflicts among the agents due to its relatively long traversal time across floors, especially when many agents need to reach a different floor. On the other hand, the planner has to reason in a larger state space including the states of the elevators, besides the locations of the agents.
tldr: This paper addresses the Multi-Agent Path Finding problem in environments with elevators, highlighting the challenges of managing conflicts and larger state spaces introduced by elevator usage.
tldr_zh: 本文探讨了包含电梯的多智能体路径规划问题 (MAPF-E)，强调了由于电梯的使用而引入的冲突管理和更大的状态空间所带来的挑战。
summary_zh: 本文研究了一个名为“多智能体路径规划与电梯”（MAPF-E）的问题，该问题旨在为多个智能体寻找从起点到终点的无冲突路径，这些起点和终点可能位于不同的楼层，并且智能体可以使用电梯在楼层之间移动。电梯的存在使得智能体之间的交互更加复杂，并给规划带来了新的挑战。一方面，由于电梯跨楼层移动的时间相对较长，尤其是当许多智能体需要到达不同的楼层时，电梯可能会导致智能体之间产生许多冲突。另一方面，除了智能体的位置之外，规划器还必须在包括电梯状态的更大的状态空间中进行推理。
